Job Description:
At MFS, you will find a culture that supports you in doing what you do best. Our employees work together to reach better outcomes, favoring the strongest idea over the strongest individual. We put people first and demonstrate care and compassion for our community and each other. Because what we do matters – to us as valued professionals and to the millions of people and institutions who rely on us to help them build more secure and prosperous futures.

THE ROLE

Applying industry and regulatory understanding, the Compliance Lead Specialist supports the Investment Team (Portfolio Managers and Traders) by analyzing compliance issues from order entry through to trade execution (Fixed Income, Equity, and Derivative Products), ensuring a high level of service to the stake holders.
Interacts with MFS management to proactively monitor and resolve regulatory and compliance issues, with an emphasis on regional regulatory and legal frameworks.
Provides input and engagement to strategic projects and process improvements to help deliver on the department’s strategic goals aligned to fixed income and derivative operations.

WHAT YOU WILL DO
Review pre- and post-trade alerts to ensure adherence to investment restrictions, mandates, and guidelines.
Develop and maintains a comprehensive understanding of investment and regulatory restrictions (e.g., Investment Management Agreement, Prospectus).
Support Portfolio Managers and Traders with timely resolution and proactive communication.
Apply understanding of fixed income, equity and derivative instruments to analyze impact to investment guidelines and regulatory requirements.
Work with Compliance management to ensure that, within a specific part of the business, the organization’s activities comply with all pertinent legal and regulatory requirements including those relating to regional APAC regulators as well as 40 Act, UCITs and NI-81-102.
Monitor and maintain portfolio restriction lists in the trading systems and platforms.
Develop, review and update procedures and controls within the group and assist management in ensuring compliance on an ongoing basis.
Examine department workflow and makes recommendations on ways to increase efficiency through automation or other means while maintaining internal controls.
Review reports and otherwise ensure the integrity of reports generated from the department.
Provide training, guidance and support to Global Compliance Department on regional regulatory requirements relating to equity, fixed income and derivatives.
Performs additional related responsibilities as required.
WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent direct experience required.
Minimum 5 years of experience in investment compliance within the financial services industry, preferably with exposure to asset management.
Understanding of relevant regulatory frameworks and guidelines within APAC jurisdictions.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret complex investment compliance requirements incorporating fixed income, equity and derivative instruments.
Ability to collaborate proactively with investment stakeholders and communicate compliance matters to non-compliance stakeholders.
Ability to foster strong interpersonal relationships and to collaborate effectively in cross-functional and international teams throughout the organization.
Proactive and meticulous attention to detail. Takes ownership and drive outcomes.
Proficient in using investment compliance systems monitoring and other relevant industry tools.
Proficiency in MS Office Suite and ability to develop proficiency in MFS systems as required.
Able to work overtime as necessary to keep current with work.
Some periodic travel, domestic and international, is required.
PREFERRED SKILLS, QUALIFICATIONS & EXPERIENCE

Direct experience using CRIMS (Charles River Investment Management Solutions).
Experience in supporting the onboarding new workflows, processes and systems to enhance operational effectiveness.

At MFS, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace. If you are excited about this role but your past experience doesn't align perfectly, we encourage you to apply - you might be just the right candidate for this role or others.

At MFS, we view our employees as family — and like you, we want our family happy and healthy. That's why we provide competitive benefits programs and family-friendly policies which support the work-life balance needs of our employees.

Job Description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION
Founded in 2011, Empact is a social enterprise with a social mission to build the capacity of social organisations in Singapore and the region. As a capacity builder to non-profits and social enterprises, we serve as an intermediary to our corporate partners where we match and manage their resources, expertises and skilled volunteers with the real needs in the social sector.
Much of the matching, in most cases require us to design and develop customised activities or programmes for a meaningful exchange or partnership between corporates and social organisations. This is to ensure a win-win for both parties towards a sustainable and effective social impact.
In the bigger scheme of our social mission, we bridge the capacity gap in the social sector with other stakeholders such as government agencies, grantmakers and volunteers to provide social organisations access to affordable (or pro bono) and professional services that are critical to their daily operations.
Over the past years, we have impacted more than 700 social organisations and channelled 8,000 volunteers to contribute to the growth of the social sector. In 2019, we were selected as a finalist for the Social Enterprise of Year by of the President Challenge. This is the highest accolade for social enterprises in Singapore.
RESPONSIBILITIES
We are currently looking for hands-on and meticulous individuals who are passionate to inspire and support corporations in their volunteering journey.
This job is for you if you are:
● Passionate about active engagement in supporting the non-profit sector and social enterprises
● Able to strive well in an action-oriented and agile environment
● Comfortable working both independently and in coordination with cross-functional teams
● Able to work on multiple projects and prioritize effectively
● Motivated to exercise and stretch your different skill sets and gain professional development
● Enthusiastic and committed in providing excellent service to clients and stakeholders
● Committed to knowledge sharing and can work with volunteers and other interns
QUALIFICATIONS
● Possess relevant experiences in any of the following:
- project management
- program coordination
- event management
- employee/community engagement
● Basic knowledge of, or some experience in marketing or communications
● Basic knowledge of, or some experience in data analysis
● Strong interpersonal skills, and verbal and written communication skills
● Have some familiarity of the community and social service sector
● Proficiency in using Google Drive Documents and Excel
OTHER INFORMATION
What We Offer
Platform to gain exposure
Opportunity for impactful contribution
Opportunity for Learning
Commitment to coaching culture
The internship position is available from May/June 2025. Preference is 6 months full time, minimum period 3 months.

Job Description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION
Care Corner Singapore Ltd was founded in 1981, in a climate of changes in the early 1980s, as economic competition in the region intensified and the rift between the wealthy and the lower-income in particular the Chinese blue-collar workers, widened.Rev Tan Tien Ser and a group of passionate individuals believed that these marginalised people with lower income and little opportunities for a formal education could be helped to realise their potential and become more competent and fulfilled persons. Through a series of early outreach initiatives, Care Corner has evolved into an organisation offering a wide range of programmes and services delivered through various social service centres located island-wide.In a bid to stay relevant to the needs of the community, Care Corner has continuously strived to offer new and innovative programmes to support those in need and the less privileged.
At Care Corner, we set out to make a difference in the lives of individuals and families every day. Our employees are our greatest asset, no matter what role we are undertaking. We offer diverse opportunities for passionate talents seeking a positive and collaborate work environment that promotes staff wellbeing and believes in staff development and growth.
Be part of an island-wide community that brings care to every corner in Singapore.
RESPONSIBILITIES
Support the operations at Youth Services and assist in the conceptualization and implementation of delivery of services to our adolescents in different settings, including community, schools and legal systems. The potential candidate will be exposed to the wide plethora of work with our adolescents to address the risks and needs they are experiencing and meet developmental milestones, needs and tasks.
Roles & Responsibilities:
1. Adolesent-Centric • Conceptualize, plan and execute programmes and activities with adolescents in the different settings (school, community, mandated programmes) • Partake in planning and conceptualizing programmes and session curriculum with the team, in community, group and one to one settings • Complete documentation and reports for programmes and sessions conducted • Take part in systemic work such as case conferences and stakeholder meetings • Handle crisis situations faced by the adolescents in reference to organisational framework and supervisory input where required
2. Research and Evaluation • Support in knowledge acquisition of the team to better our service quality and efficacy • Support in research project pieces that help to advance our understanding of adolescent needs and delivery of programmes in the various settings • Partake in research and evaluation of programmes run by the service group • Represent the department in interfacing with stakeholders and partners in different settings • Assist in management of stakeholders and crafting of collaterals for presentation and sharing
3. Learning and Development • Actively learn and provide inputs during learning and development segments held by the department and service group level • Take initiative in proposing the learning and development needed to support own development to gain competency in carrying out different functions needed for the work • Partake in learning moments with colleagues in Youth Services
Requirements:
Degree in Social Work
Preferably some experience in working with young persons; Entry level graduates are welcome to apply
Able to engage with young persons in both formal and informal settings
Able to work independently to conceptualize, design, deliver and evaluate programmes
Able to hold conversations with young persons and external partners and partake in sports and physical activities when needed
Comfortable working in multi-locations and varying working hours

Job Description:
The Senior Client Project / Project Manager is an advanced subject matter expert, responsible for leading and directing concurrent client projects classified as standard or complex.

This role may also be involved in larger-scale programs and is responsible for delivery across multiple business areas, geographies and technologies.

The primary responsibility of the Senior Client Project / Program Manager is to interface with all project stakeholders to take projects from original concept through to final implementation, including handing over to Operations (whether internal to organization or to the client's support operations).

This role may take responsibility for the management of projects and programs within more complex/partnership accounts and collaborate and engage with senior members involved in program delivery at the client site.

In this instance, the Senior Client Project / Program Manager leads the coordinated management of multiple related projects within a program to achieve the business objectives and ensure the strategic benefits are transitioned to the client organization.

What You'll Be Doing

Key Responsibilities:

· Leads and directs concurrent standard or complex projects and in the case of program management, ensure the management of multiple related projects directed towards a common objective.
· Engages with stakeholders to deliver projects from original concept through final implementation.
· Ensures client satisfaction and manage escalations, acting as a single point of contact to the client.
· Ensures that the project/program delivers an as-sold solution, remains within the baselined budget and is delivered on time whilst maintaining quality criteria and client satisfaction.
· Manages the delivery of the project/program, including rigorous scope control and change management.
· Ensures client satisfaction and manage escalations, acting as a single point of contact to the client
· Documentation and management of risks and issues.
· Ensures clear and concise communications to all stakeholders.
· Provides pre-sales support by working with sales teams to scope and cost a project or program solution which includes the completion of a proposal. This may include Client presentations of our delivery approach as part of a tender process.
· Identifies opportunities and influences the sale by conducting a business conversation with the client positioning organization's consulting and technical services offerings.
· Coordinates activities of the project teams through task delegation, resource assignment and program management.
· Performs any other related task as required.
· This role involves regular interaction with stakeholders and end users in Japan therefore, the candidate must be proficient in Japanese for effective business communication.

Knowledge and Attributes:
· Advanced project management skills, including the ability to plan, organize, and execute projects from initiation to completion.
· Ability to establish strong relationships with internal stakeholders and external clients.
· Ability to manage customer satisfaction, commitment, and expectations to high service levels and manage escalations adequately.
· Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
· Excellent team-building skills and ability to work in high-pressure environments.
· Advanced ability to manage urgent and complex tasks simultaneously.
· Advanced business acumen and commercial skills.
· Passionate, strong initiative, self-driven with a commitment to succeed.
· Advanced influencing ability whilst taking a collaborative approach.
· Decisive with good attention to detail ability.
· Advanced ability to promote project services to both internal stakeholders and external clients.
· Excellent competency in project change management.
· Extended knowledge of the industry, domain, or technology related to the client's project.
· Advanced problem-solving and analytical skills to identify and address issues that may arise during project implementation.

Academic Qualifications and Certifications:
· Bachelor's degree or equivalent in business and / or project management or related field.
· Relevant project management certifications preferably PMP beneficial.
· ITIL certification is beneficial.

Required Experience:
· Advanced demonstrated project/program management experience preferably in a multinational Professional Services environment.
· Advanced client engagement experience.
· Advanced demonstrated understanding of the project/program life cycle.
· Advanced demonstrated competency in project/program change management.

Job Description:
About AlphaSense:
The world’s most sophisticated companies rely on AlphaSense to remove uncertainty from decision-making. With market intelligence and search built on proven AI, AlphaSense delivers insights that matter from content you can trust. Our universe of public and private content includes equity research, company filings, event transcripts, expert calls, news, trade journals, and clients’ own research content.
The acquisition of Tegus by AlphaSense in 2024 advances our shared mission to empower professionals to make smarter decisions through AI-driven market intelligence. Together, AlphaSense and Tegus will accelerate growth, innovation, and content expansion, with complementary product and content capabilities that enable users to unearth even more comprehensive insights from thousands of content sets. Our platform is trusted by over 4,000 enterprise customers, including a majority of the S&P 500. Founded in 2011, AlphaSense is headquartered in New York City with more than 2,000 employees across the globe and offices in the U.S., U.K., Finland, India, Singapore, Canada, and Ireland. Come join us!

About The Role

We are looking for an entrepreneurial, driven, and accomplished Strategic Associate Account Executive, focused on assisting execute an enterprise growth strategy in partnership with a global sales team devoted to our largest global client relationships in the Financial Services market. This is an opportunity to join a high-growth company and sell an award-winning product that is rapidly getting adopted across the market internationally, and this person will be able to make a significant contribution to the company’s growth and future success.

Who You Are
Prior sales experience with a B2B SaaS solution and a customer network in the Financial Services market (buy side and sell side).
Proven track record of exceeding targets; we maintain a team of top sales performers and a great winning culture. Reputation for consistently delivering results and setting the example for work ethic, initiative, enthusiasm, and commitment.
Ability to listen actively, ask insightful questions, and uncover the real needs of the client, becoming a trusted partner. Use your knowledge of the industry, competitive environment, and client experiences to identify trends and stay ahead of customer needs.
Partner with the global sales team to help execute a strategic client growth plan with the global sales team for your book of accounts across a shared revenue target.
Able to cold call and prospect across a client base while building a pipeline.
Partner with our Global Customer Success, Sales Development and Product Specialist teams to deliver strong growth across a defined client base.
Conduct product demonstrations in a value-based sales environment.
Quick learner and able to implement feedback effectively.
An energetic and creative individual, possessing natural curiosity with the ability to learn quickly. Must have a strong work ethic.
Strong ability to develop rapport with new people, and to maintain relationships, combined with a positive, outgoing personality.
Outstanding communicator, whether in presentations or casual conversations, and can convey value propositions clearly across all levels of an organization.
Able to distill and explain complex issues in simple terms.
Experience hitting six-figure or seven-figure revenue targets on an annual basis.
Able to travel to clients on a regular basis.
What You'll Do
Partner with the global sales team to help execute a strategic client growth plan with the global sales team for your book of accounts across a shared revenue target.
This role is focused on upselling existing client relationships, requiring you to research opportunities, initiate discussions, prospect new teams, conduct demos, manage product trials, and close sales.
Develop strong internal relationships, garnering buy in from those you partner with and marshaling resources to deliver on revenue outcomes.
Coordinate with your team to forecast accurately and develop the necessary pipeline to meet and exceed goals.
Work closely with our Product Specialist team during trials to ensure client engagement
Gather and pass on information on market and client product needs to sales, product management, content, and product marketing to help us continually enhance our products.
AlphaSense is an equal-opportunity employer. We are committed to a work environment that supports, inspires, and respects all individuals. All employees share in the responsibility for fulfilling AlphaSense’s commitment to equal employment opportunity. AlphaSense does not discriminate against any employee or applicant on the basis of race, color, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, religion, marital status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, military or veteran status, disability, or any other non-merit factor. This policy applies to every aspect of employment at AlphaSense, including recruitment, hiring, training, advancement, and termination.
In addition, it is the policy of AlphaSense to provide reasonable accommodation to qualified employees who have protected disabilities to the extent required by applicable laws, regulations, and ordinances where a particular employee works.

Recruiting Scams and Fraud
We at AlphaSense have been made aware of fraudulent job postings and individuals impersonating AlphaSense recruiters. These scams may involve fake job offers, requests for sensitive personal information, or demands for payment. Please note:
AlphaSense never asks candidates to pay for job applications, equipment, or training.

If you believe you’ve been targeted by a scam or have any doubts regarding the authenticity of any job listing purportedly from or on behalf of AlphaSense please contact us. Your security and trust matter to us.

Grounded in the belief that all PWIDs have innate abilities and talents, MINDS is committed to empowering them and enhancing their quality of life, while advocating for greater inclusivity for PWIDs as a nation.
MINDS is today one of the largest charities in Singapore employing more than 850 staff. With the key focus to expand community-based services, improving the employability of PWIDS, strengthening education, and engendering inclusive community living, MINDS offers a holistic range of services and programmes across the PWIDs' lifespan.

Job Description:
Portcast is Singapore-based B2B SaaS platform providing real time vessel and container visibility, tracking and dynamic predictive analytics for LSPs, LogTech, Shippers & BCOs. We’re focused on building the next-gen visibility platform to predict how cargo moves across the world and enable data-driven decisions. Backed by some of the major investors in the tech industry, we believe that the logistics industry is at the inflection point of large-scale digitization.

We are a team of ambitious industry experts, data storytellers and passionate engineers. We offer a fast-paced, innovative environment where you will be empowered to sell our AI-product to C-level executives. We are customer-obsessed and are constantly working to provide our customers access to actionable and insightful data to build resilient supply chains.

By leveraging proprietary machine learning algorithms and real-time external market data, Portcast empowers its clients achieve real-time visibility, reduce operational costs and improve customer experience, thereby improving supply chain profitability. Our proprietary data analytics engine is custom-designed for the logistics industry.

About the role:
We are seeking a sales process-driven, customer-centric, and results-oriented Sales Manager or Account Executive with a strong track record in B2B/enterprise sales. In this individual contributor hunter role focused entirely on new business acquisition, you will drive predictable sales growth by building and converting a robust pipeline of global prospects into long-term customers.

What success looks like in this role:
• Consistently meet or exceed the company’s quarterly and annual sales goals through your ambitious approach in Sales
• A consistent and growing stream of clients opting for paid trials and subsequently becoming customers
• Our customer journey is frictionless and customers are promoters and advocates

What You’ll Do:
Develop and execute a strategic sales plan to expand our customer base globally, with a focus on Asia, and solidify our market presence, primarily focusing on acquiring new logos.
Manage end-to-end sales from prospecting, qualification, customer research and demo meetings to negotiations & closing SaaS contracts with senior executives in B2B companies.
Proactively hunt for new customers and nurture leads, leveraging personal network, proactive lead generation efforts, and market insights.
Actively grow pipeline including planning and forecasting of sales for your targets.
Collaborating with marketing and product teams to refine sales assets and product offerings.
Work closely with Customer Success teams to ensure smooth handover of accounts after closing, facilitating a seamless transition for trial management and ongoing customer support.
Provide regular updates on sales metrics, pipeline status, challenges, and support needs, while also offering insights for product improvements.
To thrive in this role, you must have:
Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, Engineering, Logistics, Supply Chain, or related fields.
5+ years of experience in enterprise B2B SaaS sales or account management, ideally with experience selling to Freight Forwarders, LSPs, BCOs. LogTech experience is a plus.
Proven ability to exceed sales quotas and navigate complex enterprise sales cycles.
A process-oriented mindset, with the ability to qualify leads, manage pipelines, and work effectively with CS, SDR, and Marketing.
Experience breaking into new regional markets (e.g., Greater China) and developing early traction.
Proven success rate at levels above quotas.
Curiosity about customer needs and product features, with a focus on digging deeper to understand client challenges and opportunities.
Self-starter mentality, comfortable working independently in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.
Excellent presentation, negotiation, and deal-closing skills, with a proven ability to engage senior executives.
The capacity to work independently, troubleshoot issues, and involve leadership only when critical issues arise.
Proficiency in Mandarin is a strong plus to support outreach and penetrate the Greater China market.
Join us at Portcast and be part of a high performing team that is shaping the future of the logistics and shipping industry through cutting-edge predictive analytics!

Job Description:

The Institute of Microelectronics (IME), a leading research institute within A*STAR, is seeking an experienced Program Manager to lead our Centre of Excellence in Advanced Packaging. This role offers a unique opportunity to drive innovation at the intersection of academia and industry in the dynamic field of semiconductor research. As a Program Manager, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of microelectronics, overseeing projects that advance the state-of-the-art in Packaging. Your leadership will be instrumental in fostering innovation, developing talent, and strengthening Singapore's position as a global leader in semiconductor technology.

Strategic Leadership

Project planning and management.

Performance Evaluation

Job Description:
This role oversees, manages and growth of the certification, membership and Fintech registry business of SFA, and maintains their accurate databases. Responsibilities include enhancing growth of these businesses, evaluating companies for certification, reviewing product documentation, conducting due diligence for membership and registry maintenance, and collaborating with cross-functional teams.

The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, proficient in collaborative tools such as Gmail, Google Docs, and Google Sheets, and can adapt to new tools and features. They should also be skilled in real-time collaboration and effective communication with team members using Google Workspace tools.

Responsibilities

FinTech Certification

● Overseeing the growth and renewal for FinTech Certification, including conducting initial consultations with interested companies to understand their business, product, and readiness for FinTech Certification
● Guide companies through the FinTech Certification process, requirements, and benefits
● Conduct detailed evaluations of companies' suitability for certification based on defined criteria
● Review and evaluate criteria for certification, including product documentation and any available user feedback or market analysis to assess the product's readiness and scheduling of product demonstration sessions with companies to evaluate the product's functionality, user interface, and the value it provides to users
● Present findings to the Approving Committee and make recommendations for approval or rejection
● Issue e-certificates to approved companies and update the certified company list on the official website or database
● Provide support to certified companies and answer any questions they might have
● Conduct regular check-ins to ensure certified companies continue to meet the certification requirements
● Organise quarterly Certification Award Presentation Ceremony to bring up the prestige, awareness and recognition of the FinTech Certification programme.

Membership & FinTech Registry

● Overseeing the growth and renewal process for both Membership & FinTech Registry, including timely marketing of promotions, communication with members and updating membership records
● Managing changes or updates to membership and registry terms, fees, or benefits
● Handling payment processing for membership and registry fees and dues, and troubleshooting payment issues
● Providing member support and serving as the main point of contact for member inquiries, including meet-the-member and visit-member-premise sessions
● Maintaining accurate membership and registry databases and generating reports on its trends
● Collaborating with cross-functional teams to ensure seamless operation of the membership and registry system
● Participating in the development of new membership programs or initiatives
● Contributing to the overall improvement of membership and registry processes and systems
● Developing and maintaining documentation outlining membership and registry policies, procedures, and guidelines.
● Advocating for a vibrant membership community by maintaining proactive member retention policies.
Desirable Skills
● Efficient in using Gmail, Google Docs, and Google Sheets for collaborative work
● Able to manage and organise email correspondence, documents, and spreadsheets effectively
● Good interpersonal, ABC sales closing and event management skills are pluses
● Skilled in creating and sharing collaborative documents and spreadsheets, enabling real-time collaboration among team members
● Proficient in using collaborative features such as commenting and real-time editing to address concerns and resolve issues quickly
● Detail-oriented with a strong focus on accuracy and completeness of collaborative documents and spreadsheets
● Able to communicate effectively and collaborate with team members using Google Workspace tools
● Comfortable with learning and adapting to new Google Workspace tools and features

Job Description:
About Grasshopper

Grasshopper is a quantitative trading technology provider based in Singapore. Our state-of-the-art technology, built from the ground up in-house, puts us at the forefront of developments in electronic trading. An unbroken record of consistency and profitability is underpinned by firm values of curiosity, empowerment and flexibility.
About Grasshopper Asset Management

Grasshopper Asset Management, a subsidiary of Grasshopper, is a Licensed Fund Management Company (LFMC) regulated by the Monetary Authority of Singapore. With low-latency quantitative strategies, we generate diversified, stable and uncorrelated returns with an unbroken 18 year streak of profitability by maintaining multiple layers of algorithmic and manual risk controls.
About the role
We are expanding and seeking for an experienced C++ Developer to join a new and ambitious HFT team with proven alphas. You will be working closely in a small team with senior Quants from top tier HFTs, with over 15 years of strong track record, and other experienced Developers. As a key contributor on the design and implementation of the trading ecosystem, you will have a direct impact on the team’s success.
As a key member of the Trading Team, your responsibilities include:
Helping design key components of a high performance scalable trading system
Utilising your expertise in C++ to implement those designs
Helping design and deploy the trading’s team research system and cloud infrastructure for large scale AI/ML research
Utilising your knowledge in python/C++ to achieve that goal
We'd love for you to have:
At least 5 years of deep expertise in C++
Proficiency in software architecture
Experience in low latency or high throughput environments
Experience in Python
The ability to write clean code
Good interpersonal and collaboration skills
What we offer:
Competitive compensation linked to the team performance
21 days annual leave
An opportunity to learn from experienced professionals, fostering mentorship opportunities and personal growth
Comprehensive Insurance Package with extended coverage for dependents
Well stocked pantry
Annual Dental & Wellness budget
Gym membership
What you can expect working at Grasshopper:
At Grasshopper, you will be working in a diverse and dynamic environment with a flat hierarchy. With 95 employees and 15 nationalities working in an open office, communication is essential to performance. To keep our edge as the “small giant” of trading technology, we give employees a high level of autonomy and encourage them to get creative, take risks, make mistakes and learn from them. The sprint is on!
Grasshopper is an equal opportunity employer.

Reporting directly to higher management, you will lead a team of Human Resource Business Partners to identify, develop, and drive key initiatives which include talent acquisition, talent/performance management, workforce and succession planning, manpower review, industrial relations as well as rewards and recognition. Your role is both strategic and hands-on, monitoring and implementing the full range of HR Business Partnering and HR Operations services to the organization, ensuring that they are effective and in accordance with the trading and manufacturing’s HR principles and policy. You will be required to work with higher management and managers to shape the people strategies, execute all HR initiatives, and drive HR excellence.
In partnership with leaders, you will provide leadership to drive strategies to achieve organizational goals. In your role, you will champion HR digital transformation projects including process improvements, and change management in trading, and manufacturing, and act as a catalyst to propel the workforce towards desired change and growth.

Provide strong business partnering management in areas such as talent acquisition, talent management, workforce planning, performance management, and staff engagement with alignment to the business strategies and contribute to the organization's goals.
Partner with the higher management and leadership team to create a culture that supports the strategy for organizational growth.
Build effective relationships with all levels of the organization, providing guidance and coaching on employee relations, performance, and development issues.
Identify opportunities provide solutions to problems and render valuable services.
Consult with leaders and managers on increasing leadership, team, and organisational effectiveness; provide fair and consistent advice on HR programs, policies, and other HR-related items.
Play a critical role in workforce planning and talent succession planning.

Good degree, preferably with a Graduate Diploma in Human Resources Management
More than 12 years of HR managerial experience, preferably in a complex large organization or MNC
Strong knowledge of HR practices and demonstrated ability to provide HR solutions in a fast-paced and complex environment. Knowledge of the trading and manufacturing industry is an advantage.
Effective teamwork and organizational skills
Possess excellent communication skills.
Able to work under pressure, multi-task, and handle diverse issues.
Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders across all levels.
Only for Singaporean / SPR

Job Description:
Job Title: Senior Technical cum DP Superintendent

This is a full-time Senior Technical cum Dynamic Positioning Superintendent position responsible with regards to safety, operations, technical, procurement, crewing and budget compliance aspects for vessels in the fleet.

1) 1-month AWS
2) Health Insurance
3) Good career growth & exposure

Key responsibilities include, but are not limited to, the following:
Safety
Active member of incident investigation, SEMM Management Review Team & the Shore Emergency Response Team as required.
Attending to machinery failures & Incidents investigation, to identify and develop root cause and corrective action plan. including issuing technical circulars if required.
Provide support for Audit’s, risk assessment and all Safety, environmental, operational and technical aspect. Responsible for issue of Management of Change (MOC) for vessel assigned as required.
Support and provide advice in technical investigation on vessel’s machinery breakdown and insurance claim. Provide advice to the investigation team in developing root cause and corrective action plans.
Visit vessel as part of the Company Senior Management to comply HSSEQ plan
Operations
Ensure vessels meet all obligations under the Charter Parties Agreements in a safe & efficient manner.
Responsible for budget preparation, maximizing Uptime, monitoring VOC (Vessel operating Cost), Insurance claims and striving to achieve cost efficiency.
Ensure legislative and regulatory compliance for vessels assigned – such as class, flag state, port state, local and international requirements and good practices.
Support in up-keep, update and maintain all vessel’s technical data in shared drive, such as equipment manuals, drawing, certificates, technical service report, maker’s circular letters, Class report etc.
Technical & Procurement Responsibilities
Ensure all vessels are well-maintained per PMS (TM Master) and manufacturers’ requirements, including updating job scopes based on operational needs.
Lead and manage the Technical and Procurement teams, including Superintendents, Procurement staff, and Technical Admin, ensuring effective collaboration and performance.
Supervise ship management, dry-docking, overhauls, and major repairs. Provide guidance on technical matters to maximize uptime and operational efficiency.
Monitor and guide the technical team in using PMS software (TM Master) effectively, ensuring maintenance tasks align with equipment specs and vessel requirements.
Respond promptly to vessel breakdowns, providing timely updates to clients and owners on repair progress and ensuring rapid resolution.
Oversee fleet DP systems, navigation/communication, and shipboard electronics, including participation in DP trials, FMEA, and related audits.
Support vessel procurement needs by sourcing, evaluating, negotiating, and approving vendors while managing cost efficiency and budget adherence.
Conduct regular vessel visits and audits, ensure superintendents perform quarterly inspections, and track closure of deficiencies from all reports and surveys.
Maintain Class, Flag, Port State, and client compliance by arranging surveys and audits, and ensuring vessels meet Charter Party technical obligations.
Ensure up-to-date records of technical manuals, equipment drawings, certifications, and technical updates are maintained in both soft and hard copy formats.
Crewing
Ensure the vessels crew are well-trained and maintaining the vessel.
Performance evaluation of the sea staff and office staff reporting directly or indirectly.
Briefing of Shipboard Senior Officers prior to joining vessels with regards to SEMM matters as required, company policies, specific charterers’ requirements.
Interview potential candidates forming part of the vessels crew
Job Requirements:
Minimum 5 years of shore-based experience in a similar technical role.
Previous experience as a Technical Superintendent with DP2/DP3 exposures. (preferred).
Qualification: Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical & Electronics Engineering)/ Marine Engineering.
Strong knowledge of vessel machinery systems, maintenance practices, DP operation/maintenance and maritime regulations.
Willingness to travel internationally for vessel inspections and maintenance whenever required.
Willingness to work outside regular business hours as required, to support vessel’s across multiple time zones.
skills: Charter, FMEA, Risk Assessment, Regulatory Compliance, Workplace Safety, Ships, Arranging, Electrical, Incident Investigation, Dynamic Positioning, Vessel Operations, Marine Engineering, Procurement, Emergency Response, Trials, Audits, Marine Operations

Location: Raffles Place CBD, Singapore
Job Type: Full-Time
Job Description:
We are seeking a dynamic and motivated Membership Consultant to join our team at our premier gym in Raffles Place CBD, Singapore. The ideal candidate will have a passion for fitness, excellent sales skills, and a customer-centric approach. As a Membership Consultant, you will be responsible for advising clients on the best membership packages and promotions to suit their needs, while also excelling in sales and contributing to the overall success of our gym.
Key Responsibilities:
Sales and Membership Consultation:
Engage with potential and existing clients to understand their fitness goals and needs.
Advise clients on the most suitable membership packages and promotions available.
Achieve and exceed monthly sales targets by converting leads into memberships.
Maintain up-to-date knowledge of membership options, promotions, and gym services.
Customer Service:
Provide exceptional customer service to all gym members and potential clients.
Handle inquiries and resolve any issues or complaints in a professional and timely manner.
Ensure a welcoming and hospitable environment for all members and visitors.
Administrative and Operational Duties:
Conduct daily administrative tasks, including maintaining accurate records and preparing reports.
Manage gym operations, such as checking in members, monitoring gym usage, and ensuring equipment is in good condition.
Assist with the organization and execution of gym events and promotional activities.
Collaborate with the management team to improve processes and enhance member experience.
Requirements:
Proven experience in a sales role, preferably within the fitness or wellness industry.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Strong customer service orientation and the ability to build relationships with clients.
Self-motivated and goal-oriented with a track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
Proficient in Microsoft Office and familiar with gym management software.
Ability to work flexible hours, including evenings and weekends, as required.
Benefits:
Competitive salary with performance-based incentives.
Access to gym facilities and complimentary membership.
Ongoing training and professional development opportunities.
Supportive and dynamic work environment.
How to Apply:
Interested candidates are invited to submit their resume and a cover letter detailing their relevant experience and why they are the ideal candidate for this position to [your email address] by [application deadline].

Job Industry: Accounting / Auditing / Taxation, Banking and Finance, Professional Services
Job Description:
We are looking for a self-driven and proactive individual to ensure the smooth functioning of eBilling operations for the Asia Pacific region, while maintaining high standards of compliance and quality. The role involves managing a local eBilling support team, overseeing eBill processing for Singapore and Australia, and providing assistance to the Hong Kong Billing team when needed. The role will act as a regional expert, offering guidance and support for all eBilling-related queries and operations in the region.

What you'll do:
Day-to-day management and supervision of local eBilling team members in Singapore & Australia.
Regularly monitor workloads and ensure output meets quality and accuracy standards.
Monitor eBills to ensure timely submission and identify any issues or delays.
Track rejection rates and identify areas for improvement to reduce the level of eBill rejections.
Provide ongoing training and development to team members to enhance their skills and efficiency.
Ensure all team members adhere to best practices for eBilling compliance guidelines, including: New Timekeepers and Rates, Matter IDs, Copy Disbursements (PDFs), Budgets / Accruals, Others
Offer support to the team with eBill processing when required.
Lead contact for Asia Pacific eBilling queries and troubleshooting.
Make recommendations for process improvements to enhance eBilling efficiency.
Review and understand Client OCG (Operating / Client Guidelines) requirements to ensure compliance.
Collaborate with the CFE (Client Finance & eBilling) and CRE (Client Relationship Executive) teams to manage and support key clients.
Document and communicate local eBilling processes to ensure consistency across the region.
Assist with local client / matter onboarding, providing necessary support for smooth integration.
Assist with local eHub integration to ensure seamless connection for eBilling processing.
Investigate and resolve any system-related issues, escalating to eHub support as necessary (consulting with Global Manager in the first instance).
Regularly assess and stay updated on eBilling systems, including Aderant and eHub functionalities, and assist in the education of the team on new developments and updates.
Participate in regular meetings with Global Manager to provide feedback, discuss complex system issues, and share regional insights.
Provide regular updates to management regarding regional eBilling performance and team activities.
Maintain open and effective communication channels with regional Billing PAs to ensure best practices are followed.
Support the Hong Kong Billing team when cover is required, ensuring no disruptions to eBilling processes.
The successful candidate will possess the following qualities:
Problem-solving: Be able to independently identify issues, proactively approach manager, and provide solutions.
Communication & interpersonal skills. This position involves liaison with clients and colleagues across all levels and countries.
Effective time management is essential. The eBilling team works in a fast paced and sometimes demanding environment, at times working against competing deadlines.
Strong knowledge of eBilling platforms: Experience with Aderant and eHub would be highly beneficial, preferably within a legal or professional services environment.
Leadership experience. Excellent leadership skills is essential as you will be managing teams from different countries.
Focus on accuracy and attention to detail. To become a reliable and trusted advisor.
Fast learner. Quick to adapt and acquire new skills, with the ability to efficiently grasp complex concepts and tools.
skills: Budgets, Leadership, Troubleshooting, Microsoft Office, Interpersonal Skills, Procurement, Compliance, Attention to Detail, Time Management, Professional Services

Job Description:
About Grasshopper
Grasshopper is a quantitative trading technology provider based in Singapore. Our state-of-the-art technology, built from the ground up in-house, puts us at the forefront of developments in electronic trading. An unbroken record of consistency and profitability is underpinned by firm values of curiosity, empowerment and flexibility.

About Grasshopper Asset Management
Grasshopper Asset Management, a subsidiary of Grasshopper, is expanding and seeking for an experienced Junior Trader to join our team and help drive the success of our trading operations in the markets we cover. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record in equity trading with intraday/day trading experience, with a deep understanding of financial markets and the ability to make informed and timely decisions.

As a key member of the Trading Team, you'll:
Develop and execute intraday trading strategies ( < 24h holding period) in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
Monitor market trends, news, and economic data to make informed trading decisions
Master and execute trading algorithmic and discretionary strategies
Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and best practices in risk management
Exercise discipline and ensure consistency by identifying and navigating trading risks and opportunities
We'd love for you to have:
Minimum 5 years of relevant trading experience in a HFT and/or professional equity trading experience with a strong track record of success, with a proven P&L record
Own or operate successful alphas/strategies
Quick and decisive thinking and the ability to perform in a dynamic and stressful environment
Knowledge of any programming language will be an added advantage i.e. Python
A strong passion for competitive activities e.g. trading, games, sports

Job Description:
Job Title: DP (Dynamic Positioning) & Technical Superintendent

DP Assurance Scope

Evaluate and ensure DP assurance process are comply by both onboard and ashore teams.
Organize FMEA/DP Trials/Survey and provide solutions to close the findings. Maintain all records relating to DP Assurance (FMEA, DP Manual, CAMO Annual trials, ASOG)
Monitor and evaluate crew DP training and competence as per the DP assurance program
Monitoring, reviewing and make recommendations on DP Requirement in conjunction with Class, Flag state, IMO, IMCA and industry standards.
Investigate, review DP incidents, and provide corrective and preventive actions
Liaise with Class and other DP competent authorities for approval
Liaise with Client and competent authorities on DP related Matters on behalf of the company
Assist in troubling shooting of DP equipment failure
Conduct regular Audits and Inspection on DP procedures and equipment onboard. Ensure DP equipment is maintained onboard
Identify Critical parts for DP system and ensure adequate DP spare are available onboard
Assist & Coordinate DP projects (New Installation and Modification)
Ship Management Scope

Monitoring and maintain shipboard PMS (Planned Maintenance System) for Hull and Machinery.
Vessel Superintendents are the first point of contact between vessel under their allocation and office for operation, technical, crewing and HSE related matters. While doing so they may take assistance from respective department Managers.
Monitor and arrange shipboard scheduled statutory Classification surveys, and where possible to arrange surveys to be harmonized.
Approve, co-ordinate and arrange supply of stores, spares and consumables, including Nautical Equipment and Publications.
Approve, co-ordinate and arrange repairs.
Implement ISM and ISO 14001 in conjunction with the Operation & HSSEQ / Asst. HSSEQ Manager.
Along with technical managers maintain vessel’s class status, arranging surveys, prepare vessels for audits, inspections as required by class, flag state, port state, clients, local and international requirements and good practices.
Along with Operation and HSSEQ department, assist vessel Masters during the audit process, OVID, 3rdparty inspection and in closing out the observations.
Vessel Superintendent with certified Internal Auditor training may be asked by HSSEQ Manager to conduct Internal ISM/ISPS/MLC Audit for certain vessels during their visit.
Coordinate and control vessel’s budget relevant to Technical aspects.
Briefing of Shipboard Senior Officers prior to joining vessels with regards to SEMM matters as required, company policies, specific charterers’ requirements.
Maintain and upkeep technical Manuals, Drawings, Catalogues from makers and yards. - Verify Documentations and Certifications of New buildings.
A member of the shore Emergency Response Team for the relevant vessels they are in charge of.
Assist in the identification of safety risk assessment and environmental aspects / impacts for vessel operations. Carry out audits and inspections as assigned.
Be part of the incident investigation team providing technical assistance as and when required by the BOI.
Discuss and plan with Technical Managers for Quarterly ship visits or as and when required. Conduct and maintain record of “Quarterly Superintendent Vessel Inspection” to comply with EMAS and Client HSE Plan.
Monitor and verify deficiencies recorded in Vessel Superintendent Ship visit report, Suitability Survey or inspection report, Client or Third-Party Inspection report, OVID, etc. are closed effectively and record maintained.
Monitor and perform assessment for Master and Chief Engineer and if have any adverse comment report to EMAS Senior Management.
Address and Report any crew grievances during their vessel visit and report to EMAS Senior Management.
Monitor Statutory Certificates, LSA/FFA Certificates, Class & flag certificates and licenses for the vessel operational requirement and ensure compliance at all times.
All other task as assigned by Supervisor

Requirements
Bachelor of Engineering (Electrical & Electronics Engineering)/ Marine Engineering
5 years in Marine Construction & Commissioning inclusive in Automation, Controls & Maintenance.
DP Operator and Maintenance/ETO Course
Short travel depends on project requirement
Required to handle works outside normal working hours due projects at different time zone
Working Location: Bugis Area
skills: Construction, Technical Assistance, Risk Assessment, Arranging, ISO, Assurance, Electrical, Incident Investigation, Dynamic Positioning, Vessel Operations, Auditor, Marine Engineering, Trials, Audits, Publications, Commissioning

Job Description:
Work with amazing people creating dishes that make you proud. Perfect if you’re looking for career progression and a fun workplace that values your contribution.

The Super Loco Group operates four Mexican restaurants in Singapore, serving up fresh, creative interpretations of classic Mexican food in a fun and festive environment. Job Description:
Are you passionate about gourmet experiences and premium products? Join our team at Ryan's Grocery (S) Pte Ltd, a leading gourmet grocery and restaurant dedicated to delivering exceptional quality and service to our discerning clientele. We are at the forefront of innovation and tradition, shaping the future of gourmet retail and dining experiences. As we expand our presence in the market, we're seeking a dynamic and creative Marketing Executive/Specialist to join our vibrant team.
Role Overview: As a Marketing Executive/Specialist, you'll play a pivotal role in shaping our brand's identity and fostering customer engagement across various channels. Reporting to the Senior Marketing Manager, you'll utilize your expertise to monitor consumer behaviour trends, generate creative ideas, and develop effective marketing strategies. Your passion for the "art" of marketing will drive the organization of creative campaigns and promotional events, strengthening our reputation and facilitating continuous business growth. The position reports to the Senior Marketing Manager.

Monitor target consumer requirements, habits, and trends, alongside competitors' market initiatives.
Brainstorm creative ideas and contribute to the development of effective marketing strategies.
Identify, develop, and execute affiliate marketing opportunities to expand brand reach.
Maintain up-to-date content and ensure a positive user experience on our ecommerce websites and digital media platforms.
Plan and execute marketing communication and promotional campaigns across various channels, including social media, email marketing, print, and retail merchandising.
Collaborate with internal teams and external vendors to produce marketing collaterals and oversee campaign execution.
Assist in the development of marketing collaterals, including retail point-of-sales materials, traditional and digital media content, PR publicity, and advertising collaterals.
Liaise internally and with external vendors to execute marketing communication and promotional campaigns.
Monitor campaign progress using various metrics and submit performance reports.
Collaborate with managers in preparing budgets and monitoring expenses.
Requirements:
Diploma or Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or related field.
Minimum 1 year of marketing experience preferred, with exposure to FMCG, Retail, or F&B industries.
Strong analytical skills and proficiency in marketing data analytics tools.
Knowledge of strategic planning principles and marketing best practices.
Proficiency in traditional and digital marketing, including SEO, social media, and ecommerce.
Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail.
Strong interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal.
Proficiency in MS Office, with knowledge of video and photo editing software preferred.
Creative mindset with a passion for innovative marketing solutions.
Join Us: Shape the future of gourmet retail and dining experiences with us! If you are a driven and creative individual with a passion for marketing excellence, we invite you to apply and be part of our dynamic team.

Job Description:
We are looking for an experienced DevOps Engineer (Infrastructure) to join our client team. The ideal candidate will have expertise in automating, managing, and improving the infrastructure to support application deployment and operations. You will work closely with development and IT teams to optimize infrastructure performance, automate workflows, and ensure the reliability and scalability of our systems.
Key Responsibilities:
Manage and optimize infrastructure on cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and on-premise environments.
Automate infrastructure provisioning, configuration, and management using tools such as Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible, or Puppet.
Build and manage continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines to support automated deployments.
Ensure high availability, reliability, and performance of infrastructure systems, identifying and addressing any issues promptly.
Implement and enforce best practices for infrastructure security, monitoring, and disaster recovery.
Collaborate with development and operations teams to integrate infrastructure and application development processes.
Support the deployment, monitoring, and maintenance of applications across multiple environments (development, staging, production).
Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to infrastructure, networks, and deployments.
Document infrastructure setups, deployment processes, and standard operating procedures.
Stay updated with the latest trends and best practices in DevOps and infrastructure management.
Requirements:
Proven experience as a DevOps Engineer or in a similar role, with a strong focus on infrastructure management.
Strong knowledge of cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and on-premise infrastructure management.
Experience with Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools, such as Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible, or Puppet.
Familiarity with containerization technologies like Docker and orchestration tools such as Kubernetes.
Hands-on experience with CI/CD tools (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI, CircleCI).
Strong knowledge of networking concepts, DNS, firewalls, VPNs, and load balancing.
Experience with monitoring tools like Prometheus, Grafana, Nagios, or New Relic.
Understanding of security best practices for infrastructure, including access controls, encryption, and patch management.
Familiarity with version control systems like Git.
Excellent problem-solving and troubleshooting skills.
Ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a team environment.
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
Relevant certifications (e.g., AWS Certified DevOps Engineer, Azure DevOps Engineer Expert) are a plus.

Job Description:
Role & Responsibilities:
Position Definition (Key Purpose of the Position):
The position’s ultimate goal is to build the brand to be the #1 Outdoor Brand in Rest of Asia. This role is to inspire, equip and enable the next generation of “Explorers” – to build an inclusive brand, to spark curiosity for adventure and to ensure they understand the latest product innovations that will help them achieve their goals.
This position is responsible to work with Marketing APAC team closely to drive consumer-centric strategy and execution within APAC Integrated Marketing process that will differentiate the brand as the world-class brand & product storytelling experiences and activations that inspire and enable creative expression in our consumer in markets enlisted above. He/she will be responsible for managing the brand strategies, GTM process, campaigns, landing brand global and APAC marketing tools and content, to connect with consumers throughout the purchase funnel, from planning to execution. This role requires a proven track record in people management, x-function collaboration, as well as in building strong brand and product marketing campaigns by leveraging the full marketing mix and delivering integrated solutions fully in line with our brand DNA and strategies.
He/she will be working closely with brand Marketing head, responsible to collaborate closely with the APAC marketing team, ROA Omni Channel business and RBE team, to ensure the markets will stay true to brand's DNA, follow the global and APAC brand guideline & campaign roadmap according to product booking, drive integrated marketing impact at brand and sales levels. Provide guidance against in market execution and coordination of plans to guide market-based brand managers.
The role’s responsibility will cover HongKong, Taiwan and APAC distributor markets, with Thailand, Singapore, Malaysia, Indonesia and the Philippines as priorities, covering Vietnam, Mongolia, Nepal and other markets’ needs on a regular basis.
He/she will:
Work together with APAC Marketing head to manage ROA GTM, seasonal brand roadmap, campaign planning, strategies, budget planning, guide ROA team on campaign executions and recap in designated markets.
Drive collaboration between Marketing / Sales / Retail / Product as well as global marketing peers to deliver and improve marketing deliverables throughout the Go To Market process.
Manage and own ROA marketing budgets to maximize ROI and achieve financial plans. Track and monitor budget on a monthly basis to ensure compliance with company approved numbers
Be the brand gate keeper, ensure creative assets, digital/social/retail/EC presentation and BTL event activation/Sports IP on brand standard
Consumer research, analysis, digital trends to fuel and support dynamic markets’ needs
Athletes & outdoor community management & support, with key markets as priority
Lead, coach and develop ROA marketing team, both from a structural and talent development standpoint.
Support other APAC related projects when needed
Key Result Areas:
Brand Stewardship:
Be the Brand Gate Keeper for ROA, create and develop brand strategy and budget with APAC marketing peers to fulfil ROA market needs, ensure regional consistency and local relevancy.
Identify the right Global & APAC campaign assets for markets’ consumer touch points to fuel demand, raise brand awareness, engagement, and conversion.
Lead, coach, support countries’ marketing team, be the value-add and indispensable brand partners.
Consumer, Market & Business Intelligence:
Share consumer insights with country teams to nurture consumer-led culture across the brand, leverage and integrate consumer insights to deliver effective consumer led campaigns and brand experiences
Work closely with VF digital hub & CI team, and other sources to identify actionable insights, opportunities and key projects that will support APD markets’ brand development.
Brand Planning & Execution Excellence:
Drive collaboration between ROA business team & APAC Marketing peers to deliver and improve marketing deliverables throughout the markets’ GTM (Go To Market ) process, ensure brand drivers & marketing key styles are aligned, ultimately to drive brand campaign execution consistency.
Guide the country’s marketing team to plan & execute seasonal campaigns with targeted KPIs, including Performance & Sportswear categories, UE in designated markets, across marketing functions including Digital (social, CLM, EC), VM, PR, Sports Marketing and Events.
Support marketplace transformation strategy, amplify L2/L2+ and other new stores’ marketing activations, drive consumer & community energy at designated retail destinations.
Help country’s marketing team to set up KPIs and ‘how to measure’ methods, guide them measure marketing KPIs & ROI based on APAC brand planning guide.
Report and recap with clear actions & follows; ensure the markets share campaign plans, recaps based on APAC brand cadences and templates.
Athletes & Community Management Support:
ROA Athletes and community strategy (work with APAC Sports MKT team)
Trail athletes & community in top countries, landing in ROA markets
Climb athletes & communities in top countries
Experience
Minimum Relevant Experience: 8+ years’ experience in brand marketing role managing full marketing mix and direct reports.
Marketing experience focusing on Hong Kong and Taiwan markets is required, and that in SEA countries is a plus.

Competency Requirements :
Behavioral Competencies -
(Please refer to VF guiding principles)
• High EQ in working with cross function/multi-cultural partners
Experience in managing complex organizational matrix (Region to markets)
People management skill and experience
Understanding of outdoor sports and business
Understanding of retail
Understanding of ROA key markets (HK, TW, Some APD markets etc)
• Excellent interpersonal and communication skills (oral and written) with team of multi-cultural background, a leader as well as a pleasant team player
• Entrepreneurial & result-driven
• Presentation skill- both written and verbal English communication skill, able to produce impactful and succinct reports, content calendar, analysis, and recommendation on action
• Optimistic and dare to take calculated risks
• Team player and the ability to work independently
• Communication and influence regionally, internally and externally with partners
• Bold and creative thinking
• Patient and resilient, able to work under pressure with multiple tasks simultaneously while meeting tight deadlines
Willing to travel

Deep understanding of The North Face brand with a passion for the outdoor industries

Consumer Culture & Insights Research Methodologies

Job Description:
Job Description

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented SAP Test Manager with a proven track record in SAP testing methodologies and a strong emphasis on S/4HANA migration projects. The ideal candidate will play a pivotal role in overseeing and managing the testing activities related to SAP software implementations, enhancements, and specifically, migrations to S/4HANA. The SAP Test Manager will demonstrate expertise in test planning, execution, and leadership for a large-scale project involving high number of users while ensuring the quality and reliability of SAP systems.

Key Responsibilities:

Test Strategy and Planning:

• Develop and implement comprehensive test strategies, with a specific focus on S/4HANA migration projects and other SAP systems like BW, SAC, Datasphere and PaPM.

• Collaborate with project managers, business analysts, and SAP functional consultants to understand the unique requirements of S/4HANA migrations.

• Define test scope, objectives, and deliverables tailored to the migration context.


Test Execution:

• Oversee the execution of test plans, ensuring that testing activities are aligned with the complexities of S/4HANA migration projects.

• Coordinate closely with SAP functional and technical teams to validate system functionality during migration.

• Conduct various types of testing, including system testing, integration testing, regression testing, and performance testing, specific to S/4HANA.

• Drive test automation for Functional and regression testing


Team Leadership:

• Lead and mentor a team of SAP testers, emphasizing expertise in S/4HANA migration testing.

• Provide guidance and support to team members, ensuring a deep understanding of the challenges and nuances associated with S/4HANA migrations.

• Conduct performance evaluations and contribute to professional development plans with a focus on migration skills.

Stakeholder Communication:

• Effectively communicate test progress, results, and other relevant information to project stakeholders, emphasizing the critical role of testing in successful S/4HANA migrations.

• Collaborate with project managers to provide accurate and timely status reports with a particular focus on migration milestones.


Continuous Improvement:

• Identify opportunities for process improvement within the testing lifecycle, particularly regarding S/4HANA migration best practices.
• Implement and share insights into testing methodologies, tools, and industry trends related to S/4HANA migration projects.

Requirements

Qualifications:

• Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field.

• Proven experience as a SAP Test Manager, with a significant focus on S/4HANA migration projects.

• Strong knowledge of SAP applications, modules, configurations, and S/4HANA migration processes.

• Experience with various testing methodologies, tools, and best practices, with an emphasis on S/4HANA migration testing.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

• Project management certification (e.g., PMP) is a plus.

• SAP certification and S/4HANA certification are highly desirable.

Key Competencies:

• Leadership and Team Management

• Test Planning and Execution, particularly for S/4HANA migrations

• Communication and Collaboration

• Problem-solving and Analytical Skills

• Attention to Detail

• Stakeholder Management
skills: Performance Testing, Interpersonal Skills, Regression Testing, System Testing, Information Technology, Test Automation, SAP, Team Leadership, Attention to Detail, Team Management, Integration Testing, Test Strategy, Stakeholder Management, Test Execution, Drive Test, Test Planning

Job Description:
We are currently looking for a Cyber Security Architect for our client. This role is responsible for planning, designing, testing, implementing, and maintaining the organization’s network security infrastructure. The individual will also evaluate the security of the organization's systems for vulnerabilities, participate in penetration tests, and conduct risk analyses on LANs, WANs, and VPNs. Additionally, they will assess the efficacy and efficiency of routers, firewalls, and comparable systems.


Job Scope:
· Provide Level 3 production support for network and security infrastructure.
· Design, develop, and implement secure system architectures.
· Embed security principles into system architecture design to mitigate risks posed by new technologies and business practices.
· Develop design artifacts spanning the design, development, and implementation phases of enterprise systems, detailing security principles and their relation to the overall enterprise architecture.
· Perform routine reviews and audits of infrastructure security systems and maintain documentation of security standards and procedures.
· Gain a thorough understanding of the organization’s technology and information systems.
· Plan, research, and design reliable, scalable, and flexible security architectures for IT projects.
· Define, create, implement, and maintain corporate security policies and procedures, ensuring compliance across all employees.
· Develop security requirements for IT assets, including routers, firewalls, LANs, WANs, VPNs, and other related network devices.
· Review and approve the installation of firewalls, VPNs, routers, servers, and IDS scanning technologies.
· Prepare cost estimates for cybersecurity measures and identify potential integration issues.
· Provide technical guidance and supervision for security teams.
· Lead security awareness programs and educational initiatives for non-IT personnel.
· Respond immediately to security-related incidents (e.g., data breaches, viruses, phishing scams).
· Update and upgrade the organization’s security systems as needed.


Job Requirements:
· Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent
· Minimum of 8 years of experience in managing IT systems, with at least 5 years of experience in financial institutions.
· Subject matter expert in Systems, Networks, and Security.
· Must have experience with Cisco (ACI, Nexus, Catalyst, DNAC, ISE, iOS, NXOS), Arista(Switches, EOS), Fortinet(Firewall, IPS), F5 (LTM, ASM, APM, TMOS), Infoblox (DNS, DHCP), Skyhigh (Proxy), Ivanti (Remote Access), Checkpoint (Firewall, MDS), and Trend Micro (IPS/IDS).
· Knowledge and/or hands-on experience with emerging technologies.
· Excellent communication, interpersonal, and logical problem-solving skills.
· Customer service-oriented and a good team player.
· Ability to work shifts and be available for on-call support during non-business hours and weekends (allowance is claimable for such hours).
· Ability to work under pressure and a commitment to issue resolution.

Thank you for your interest!

Job Highlights
Attractive remuneration, staff perks and discounts
Collaborative environment with supportive colleagues and teams
Structured and comprehensive in-house material and teaching guides by our Curriculum Team
Job Description
Empower students for the classroom and beyond. We’re actively hiring for 2025 - join our team today!
The Write Connection (TWC) specialises in English and Writing classes for primary and secondary level students. We comprise over 100 professionals dedicated to one mission: to nurture generations of critical yet compassionate thinkers. We are committed to delivering first-class, well-rounded English classes to our customers.
If you have experience teaching English and want to customise your class arrangements so that they work best for your schedule, join us as a Flexi-Adjunct Teacher!
Job Responsibilities
Teach small class sizes of up to 10 students.
Conduct engaging and enriching English lessons, with structured materials written by our in-house Curriculum experts.
Foster a safe and positive learning environment, embodying a growth mindset to guide and nurture students.
Meticulously mark and return students’ work on time. Keep track of their performance and provide constructive feedback to facilitate improvement.
Build rapport with parents and update them on their child’s academic and personal progress.
Job Requirements
At least 1 year experience teaching English and/or writing in a school or learning centre.
A Degree from a reputable university, preferably in related fields of Law, Arts, Humanities, Social Sciences or Communications.
Excellent academic track record, particularly in English and General Paper (GP).
Passionate about teaching English and writing to primary and secondary school students.
Willing to develop skills and implement strategies to teach and support students of differing levels and competencies.
Willing to adopt TWC's ethos and pedagogy.
Good interpersonal and communications skills to effectively engage all stakeholders.
Excellent work ethic.
Able to teach and mark effectively, managing the weekly workload of your assigned classes.
Able to commit to classes until the end of the academic year (in December).
Remuneration will be commensurate with experience and number of classes contracted for.
What we offer
Flexibility to choose your working days and classes, provided you can commit to the full year for each class.
Competitive remuneration rates: $280 to $744 per class (*range is based on one class per month, depending on experience, class level, and class size - excludes performance bonus.)
Attractive performance bonus.
An excellent classroom environment equipped with effective technology to enhance your teaching experience.
In-house materials with guiding notes, developed by our professional Curriculum team.
A dynamic and collaborative environment with friendly colleagues and supportive teams, who are always ready to share their expertise and suggestions.
Professional development through meetings (optional for Flexi-Adjunct Teachers) on lesson preparation, student cases, and various teacher training sessions.
How to Apply
Interested candidates, please send your resume and academic transcripts to recruitment@thewriteconnection.com.sg.
What to expect after applying
1. Application Acknowledgment:
Once you submit your application, the hiring team will review your resume to shortlist candidates. This process can take up to 7 working days. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
2. Interview:
If your application is successful, you will receive a call/ text/email from us.
All shortlisted applicants will be required to complete an in-person competency assessment and interview.
All information collected will be kept in strict confidentiality and used for recruitment purposes only.
