Job Description:
Mandatory Skills
Microsoft SQL Server
Preferred Skills
Microsoft SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS), Microsoft SQL Server Reporting Services(SSRS), Azure SQL Database, MongoDB
Brief Job Description
Microsoft SQL Server (MSSQL) Administrator and MongoDB Administrator
Microsoft SQL Server (MSSQL) Administrator Responsibilities:
• Install and configure SQL Server instances, including clustering and high availability setups.
• Design efficient database schemas, indexes, and stored procedures.
• Optimize queries and performance tuning.
• Implement automated backup strategies (full, differential, log backups).
• Ensure disaster recovery and high availability (HA) using replication, mirroring, Always On Availability Groups.
• Manage user roles, permissions, and authentication mechanisms.
• Implement encryption and data masking techniques.
• Use tools like SQL Profiler, Performance Monitor, and Extended Events to diagnose and fix slow queries.
• Set up failover clustering, log shipping, replication, and Always On features.
• Address database corruption, deadlocks, and performance bottlenecks.
• Develop scripts using PowerShell and T-SQL for automated maintenance tasks.
• Manage migrations between different SQL Server versions and cloud environments (Azure SQL).

MongoDB Administrator Responsibilities:
• Install and configure MongoDB instances (standalone, replica sets, sharded clusters).
• Design efficient NoSQL schemas using collections, documents, and indexing strategies.
• Configure backup strategies (mongodump, snapshots) and recovery plans.
• Manage user roles, authentication (LDAP, x.509), and encryption.
• Use tools like mongostat, mongotop, and Atlas monitoring for performance optimization.
• Set up and maintain replica sets for high availability.
• Configure sharding for handling large datasets.
• Optimize MongoDB queries using indexes and aggregation pipelines.
• Automate administrative tasks using scripts in Python, Bash, or JavaScript.
• Analyze logs (mongod.log) and resolve issues like slow queries, connection failures.
• Manage MongoDB instances on cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, MongoDB Atlas).
• Implement CI/CD pipelines with MongoDB integrations.
skills: X509, MongoDB, Azure, Failover, T-SQL, High Availability, Replication, Log Shipping, SQL, SQL Server, Python, Performance Tuning, Encryption, SSIS, Disaster Recovery

Job Description:
Our team at A*STAR Skin Research Labs (A*STAR SRL) is committed to advancing next generation biophotonics technologies to tackle key national healthcare challenges. We offer access to cutting-edge biophotonics tools, collaboration with leading clinicians and industry partners, and opportunities for meaningful clinical and commercial translation.

We are currently seeking a scientist to contribute to the development of cutting edge miniaturized optical spectroscopy platform with innovative AI-analytics for skin health applications.

Job scope/responsibilities:
Development of optical hardware and systems for diffuse optical spectroscopy & imaging (DOSI), and Raman spectroscopy
Develop AI models for skin imaging and analytics
Develop tissue optics model for improved light-tissue interaction analysis.
Development and optimization of algorithms for spectral and image data processing, including quantitative analysis of tissue parameters
Testing of developed spectroscopy system in phantoms and later in clinical settings
Prepare and review project status, reports and ensure timely completion of planned objectives
Collaborate seamlessly with multidisciplinary teams, including clinicians, biologists, and engineers, to validate the developed system in clinical environments.
Present study concepts, experimental results, new findings to the management team and the research team members
Write peer-reviewed papers, technical disclosures, contribute to grant writing and present to internal and external stakeholders, where necessary
Job requirements:
PhD degree in Physics/Biomedical Engineering/Optical Engineering, Electrical Engineering or relevant fields with minimum 1-3 years of relevant work experience
Strong background in tissue optics modelling and spectroscopic techniques, particularly diffuse Reflectance and Raman Spectroscopy.
Familiar with AI models, AI assisted image reconstruction and analysis
Proficiency in image and spectral analysis, including preprocessing and feature extraction techniques
Experience on AI algorithm development for image enhancement
Proficient in Matlab, Python and/or LabVIEW programming, Solid works, Zemax, ANSYS
Demonstrated track record of high impact peer-reviewed journal publications
Strong communication, writing, and presentation skills.
Industry working experience is advantageous

Job Description:
Job Description

We are seeking an experienced and detail-oriented SAP Test Manager with a proven track record in SAP testing methodologies and a strong emphasis on S/4HANA migration projects. The ideal candidate will play a pivotal role in overseeing and managing the testing activities related to SAP software implementations, enhancements, and specifically, migrations to S/4HANA. The SAP Test Manager will demonstrate expertise in test planning, execution, and leadership for a large-scale project involving high number of users while ensuring the quality and reliability of SAP systems.

Key Responsibilities:

Test Strategy and Planning:

• Develop and implement comprehensive test strategies, with a specific focus on S/4HANA migration projects and other SAP systems like BW, SAC, Datasphere and PaPM.

• Collaborate with project managers, business analysts, and SAP functional consultants to understand the unique requirements of S/4HANA migrations.

• Define test scope, objectives, and deliverables tailored to the migration context.


Test Execution:

• Oversee the execution of test plans, ensuring that testing activities are aligned with the complexities of S/4HANA migration projects.

• Coordinate closely with SAP functional and technical teams to validate system functionality during migration.

• Conduct various types of testing, including system testing, integration testing, regression testing, and performance testing, specific to S/4HANA.

• Drive test automation for Functional and regression testing


Team Leadership:

• Lead and mentor a team of SAP testers, emphasizing expertise in S/4HANA migration testing.

• Provide guidance and support to team members, ensuring a deep understanding of the challenges and nuances associated with S/4HANA migrations.

• Conduct performance evaluations and contribute to professional development plans with a focus on migration skills.

Stakeholder Communication:

• Effectively communicate test progress, results, and other relevant information to project stakeholders, emphasizing the critical role of testing in successful S/4HANA migrations.

• Collaborate with project managers to provide accurate and timely status reports with a particular focus on migration milestones.


Continuous Improvement:

• Identify opportunities for process improvement within the testing lifecycle, particularly regarding S/4HANA migration best practices.
• Implement and share insights into testing methodologies, tools, and industry trends related to S/4HANA migration projects.

Requirements

Qualifications:

• Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field.

• Proven experience as a SAP Test Manager, with a significant focus on S/4HANA migration projects.

• Strong knowledge of SAP applications, modules, configurations, and S/4HANA migration processes.

• Experience with various testing methodologies, tools, and best practices, with an emphasis on S/4HANA migration testing.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

• Project management certification (e.g., PMP) is a plus.

• SAP certification and S/4HANA certification are highly desirable.

Key Competencies:

• Leadership and Team Management

• Test Planning and Execution, particularly for S/4HANA migrations

• Communication and Collaboration

• Problem-solving and Analytical Skills

• Attention to Detail

• Stakeholder Management

HERE’S A SHORT INTRO OF WHO WE ARE
Since 2005, finexis has been more than just a financial services provider – we are lifelong confidantes, guiding clients through every stage of their financial journey. As the largest and fastest growing independently-owned financial advisory firm in Singapore, we are proud to have over 900 consultants and staff, $1 billion in assets under advisory, and strategic partnerships, that establish us as a market leader in personalised financial services.

We deliver tailored financial solutions in wealth management, investment planning, and risk management, all driven by our mission to impact lives beyond finance.

WHAT TO EXPECT
The Advisory & Practice Standards department is seeking a Head of Department to lead and executes the department’s strategy to ensure the firm balances compliance with applicable regulations and business requirements. The department ensures all regulatory requirements are met, thus enabling the business to operate smoothly and effectively.

This individual should understand the business's ‘pulse’ and the wider industry ecosystem in which the firm operates. He/She will need to be familiar with regulatory thinking and expectations. You will also need to stay abreast of evolving regulatory requirements and best practices while maintaining an open mind to accepting new ways of working. To achieve this, the department works across internal silos, across the first, second and third-line functions. You will be required to establish robust reporting and escalation frameworks, receive support from the board, senior management, and managers, and have the authority to manage communication with supervisors.

WHAT YOU’LL DO
Business Support:
Spearhead and collaborate with various teams to understand business challenges and bridge regulatory requirements thereby ensuring alignment to organisational objectives
Transform complex regulatory jargon into clear, easy-to-understand language that can be seamlessly executed at operational working level
Adopt a strategic approach which allows deconstructing complex issues into clear, actionable solutions with the focus on key priorities that drives business growth
Marketing Clearance:
Establish the marketing clearance framework and SOPs on all social media platforms to ensure alignment and compliance to the company’s guidelines
Develop a comprehensive playbook for business and internal stakeholders that allows for process standardisation, guidance and best practices for decision-making
Oversee a team and ensure a comprehensive review of all marketing materials from internal stakeholders and agency leaders while still maintaining accountability
Policy Development and Implementation:
Establish the sales and advisory practices to ensure that it complies with the regulatory requirements and not compromising the efficiency of business operations
Govern the conduct of the appointed representatives and organisation leaders and set the policy standards and ensuring adherence to the guidelines
WHO YOU ARE
At least 8 years’ relevant experience in the insurance industry or financial services industry
Highly disciplined, independent, organised, result-oriented and analytical
Self-driven with a proven ability to work in a dynamic environment and a proven track record of exceeding goals and objectives
Strong interpersonal skills to manage various stakeholders
Thrive in a fluid, dynamically changing environment

WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU
Competitive remuneration
Flexi-Benefits
Hybrid work week
A workplace environment that fosters collaboration and interactions with others
Opportunity to work with various teams

Familiarity with FX/treasury infrastructure or VASP regulations

Job Description:
Join our team to play a pivotal role in mitigating tech risks and upholding operational excellence, driving innovation in risk management.

As a Tech Risk & Controls Lead in Cybersecurity and Technology Controls , you will be responsible for identifying, and mitigating compliance and operational risks in line with the firm's standards. You will also provide subject matter expertise and technical guidance to technology-aligned process owners, ensuring that implemented controls are operating effectively and in compliance with regulatory, legal, and industry standards. By partnering with various stakeholders, including Product Owners, Business Control Managers, and Regulators, you will contribute to the reporting of a comprehensive view of technology risk posture and its impact on the business. Your advanced knowledge of risk management principles, practices, and theories will enable you to drive innovative solutions and effectively manage a diverse team in a dynamic and evolving risk landscape.

Job responsibilities
• Ensure effective identification, quantification, communication, and management of technology risk, focusing on root cause analysis and resolution recommendations
• Develop and maintain robust relationships, becoming a trusted partner with LOB technologists, assessments teams, and data officers to facilitate cross-functional collaboration and progress toward shared goals
• Execute reporting and governance of controls, policies, issue management, and measurements, offering senior management insights into control effectiveness and inform governance work
• Proactively monitor and evaluate control effectiveness, identify gaps, and recommend enhancements to strengthen risk posture and regulatory compliance

Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills
• Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, Data Science, or related disciplines
• 5+ years of experience or equivalent expertise in technology risk management, information security, or related field, emphasizing risk identification, assessment, and mitigation
• Oversee and enhance cybersecurity risk management and control frameworks, leveraging extensive experience in payment processing to ensure robust protection and compliance.
• Familiarity with risk management frameworks, industry standards, and financial industry regulatory requirements
• Proficient knowledge and expertise in data security, risk assessment & reporting, control evaluation, design, and governance, with a proven record of implementing effective risk mitigation strategies
• Demonstrated ability to influence executive-level strategic decision-making and translating technology insights into business strategies for senior executives

Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills
• In-depth knowledge of the financial services industry and their IT systems

Job Industry: Environment / Health, Risk Management, Sciences / Laboratory / R&D
Job Description:
Come Join Us
EcoCaraga is a nature-based carbon project development company headquartered in Singapore. It originates, designs, and develops nature-based carbon projects across Southeast Asia that mitigate climate change through the protection of tropical forest landscapes, and the removal of greenhouse gases from the atmosphere through the restoration of forest and other natural ecosystems, while ensuring full compliance with social and environmental safeguards. EcoCaraga aims toachieve these goals through global carbon and climate markets, thus bringing value back to maintain the natural ecosystems, biodiversity, and local communities that are stakeholders in our projects. EcoCaraga draws on a talented and multidisciplinary team of experts to deliver a comprehensive approach to project design and development, resulting in high-quality and high-integrity projects brought to the market. Using market-based solutions to conservation in Southeast Asia, we work to leverage natural capital as a viable opportunity for local economic development, while helping companies meet their climate targets through nature-based carbon offsets.
Position Overview
The Carbon Lead is a core member of our technical development team in Singapore, traveling regularly to our project sites in Southeast Asia, and other locations as necessary to meet and work with our local in-country implementing teams and project stakeholders, and is directly involved in the design and development of our projects. The Lead ensures the rigorous compliance of our projects to the requirements of Verra’s Verified Carbon Standard (VCS; https://verra.org/programs/verified-carbon-standard/), with emphasis on methodologies for the robust quantification and monitoring of carbon emission reductions and removals, and the processes for validation and verification. The Lead will work directly with our Chief Technical Officer, in collaboration with our core technical development team, with our in-country implementing teams, and project partners and collaborators across the region.
Position Responsibilities
Leadership
Provide leadership within the core technical development team pertaining to the requirements of VCS methodologies on the quantification and monitoring of carbon emission reductions and removals, and support the team on relevant processes for validation and verification.
Collaborate with the core technical development team to help design and shape project activities that lead to credible and real carbon emission reductions and removals on the ground.
Develop and conduct training programs for core technical development team, local country-office teams, and project partners, thereby enhancing their technical skills and capacities in carbon-related initiatives.
Undertake research and review of projects around the world, ensuring that best practices are learned and integrated into our projects.
Utilize carbon industry expertise and understanding to identify and explore opportunities for potential partnerships or projects aligned with climate change mitigation objectives.
Project Design Oversight
Design, develop, and execute carbon-related data collection and accounting protocols and activities in and around our project sites and coordinate with local country teams accordingly to lead the implementation of the process.
Ensure the rigorous compliance of the projects to the VCS requirements, with emphasis on methodologies for the robust quantification and monitoring of carbon emission reductions and removals, and the processes for validation and verification.
Lead quality assessment and control of projects against high-integrity standards by developing quality assurance and control procedures and ensuring their implementation.
Provide training, direction, and advisory support to core technical development staff, local country teams, and project partners to ensure an understanding of requirements, frameworks, and trainings for carbon data collection, analysis, and monitoring.
Eligibility Requirements
Master’s degree or PhD or a minimum of 7 years of experience in forestry, ecology, environmental science, natural resource management, climate change, working on carbon accounting and management in the forestry and land use sector or a related field.
Experience in the development of AFOLU projects and/or programs in the voluntary and/or compliance markets using the Verra Standards is considered a distinct advantage.
Experience in conducting forest biomass inventories, soil sampling in different ecosystems, and field data collection in rural areas, and working in remote locations.
Strong knowledge in forest, mangrove, and/or peatland ecology and ecosystems, their carbon dynamics, and the impacts of human activities on these ecosystems.
Strong demonstrable skills working in both qualitative and quantitative analysis, with emphasis on carbon accounting and modeling.
Proficiency in using remote sensing, GIS, and other relevant geospatial tools applied to carbon data analysis and mapping.
Excellent communication and cross-cultural skills, with the ability to foster relationships and engage with diverse stakeholders.
Excellent command of the English language; fluency in Tagalog, Cebuano, Bahasa Indonesia, and/or Thai is considered an asset.
Ability and willingness to travel regionally around Southeast Asia.
What We Bring to You
A uniquely on-the ground experience where you will be working on real-world projects in remote locations with indigenous peoples and local communities around the region.
A multidisciplinary, multicultural, and deeply dedicated and energetic team in nature-based carbon project development.
Full health insurance coverage.
Collective team-based performance-based bonuses.
Work Condition
The candidate will primarily be based out of the EcoCaraga Singapore office, with regular travel to project sites within the region as required. The candidate will be expected to spend more than 50% of the time in the project sites.
Instructions to Applicant
Apply by sending us your CV to: jobs@ecocaraga.com indicating what position you are applying to in the subject line and a brief introduction to yourself in your message. Deadline for applications is on 18 April 2025, or as soon as a suitable candidate for the position is found. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.

Job Description:
Position Overview:
FirstCom Academy is seeking a dedicated and experienced Full-Time Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will be responsible for delivering high-quality training, ensuring the effective learning and assessment of adult learners. This role requires a commitment to maintaining assessment integrity, and contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will have a passion for teaching and a commitment to fostering a culture of excellence.
Key Responsibilities:
Training Delivery:
Conduct various courses offered by FirstCom Academy.
Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with FirstCom’s curriculum and learning standards.
Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress.
Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods.
Assessment Integrity:
Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies.
Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated.
Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.”
Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency.
Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity.
Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered.
Administrative Responsibilities:
Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner.
Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data.
Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards.
Learner Support and Engagement:
Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve.
Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment.
Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students.
Compliance and Reporting:
Adhere to FirstCom’s policies, WSQ standards, and regulatory requirements.
Participate in ongoing professional development to stay current with educational trends and best practices.
Report any non-compliance or assessment integrity issues to management promptly.
Collaboration and Teamwork:
Collaborate effectively with other trainers, curriculum developers, and operational teams to ensure a cohesive learning experience.
Attend team meetings and professional development sessions to improve teaching methods.
Requirements:
Education:
ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required.
Experience:
At least 2 years of experience in training and assessment, preferably in adult education or a related field.
Experience in both classroom and digital learning environments is preferred.
Skills:
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Excellent organizational and time management skills.
Proficient in using digital tools and online platforms for teaching and assessments.
Ability to engage, motivate, and manage adult learners.
Personal Attributes:
Passionate about teaching and committed to delivering high-quality education.
Strong attention to detail, especially in marking assessments and maintaining integrity.
Ability to adapt teaching methods to meet diverse learner needs.
High level of integrity and professionalism in all aspects of work.

Job Description:
The CareText Service Executive in the Crisis Support Team handles incoming texts from our clients in our round-the-clock Care Text service, a text-based service that provides emotional support for those in distress.
He/she decides the required follow up actions, which typically include referring of cases to the Clinical Team for crisis support or counselling. He or she provides frontline support to volunteers who are manning the organization’s Care Text service.

Duties and Responsibilities:
Conduct initial assessment and classify text messages according to the risk levels. Scope of work includes:
Classify text messages on the system
Make outgoing text messages where necessary to facilitate assessment
Provide first level response to low-risk clients where necessary
Provide information on SOS services or on community resources to third party callers
Support in the administration and documentation of the processes
Refer cases to the Clinical team for follow up actions/ crisis support interventions
Coordinate on the activation of emergency support where applicable
Frontline support to volunteers (SAMs) who are manning the organization’s Care Text Service. Scope of work includes:
Assist with care text duties in the event of shortage of volunteers
Being activated onsite to support persons in crisis or next-of-kins
Support in training and supervision of trainee volunteers
Orientate and guide new volunteers who have just started performing messaging duties on their own
Support and guide volunteers in the Phoneroom or over online platforms by monitor chats and provide consultation where appropriate
Conduct debrief sessions with volunteers where necessary, especially for those who handled text with imminent risks and experienced distress as a result
Update volunteers on clients’ development
Provide off-site support for volunteers outside of office hours where necessary
Educational Qualification:
Diploma or equivalent in Social Work, Psychology or in Counselling (preferred)
Relevant Experience:
On-the-job training available
At least 1-2 years of experience in the Social Service Sector (preferred)
Understands the Social Service landscape (preferred)
Experience in volunteer management or volunteered with other SSAs or hotline operation (preferred)
Behaviour Traits & Attitude:
Self-motivated
Good team player
Possess good interpersonal communication skills
Willing to perform shift work which includes evenings, overnights, weekends and on public holidays (mandatory)
Strong analytic and problem solving skills (preferred)

Shift Schedules:

Part-Time CareText Executive (20 hours):
Work schedule consists of 20 hours per week, inclusive of weekends and public holidays. Staff will be rostered to work on the following shifts:
(A) 10am - 2pm, (B) 5pm - 9pm, or (C) 9pm - 1am. Shifts will be done remotely.

Job Description:
About Us:
Vector InfoTech is a forward-thinking organisation building AI-powered solutions using advanced large language models (LLMs) like Llama 4 and DeepSeek. We’re seeking interns to tackle high-impact projects, with a focus on deploying LLMs to solve real-world challenges in HR, finance, and operations.

Internship Overview:
Interns will work exclusively on one project for the duration of the internship, ensuring deep engagement and ownership. You’ll collaborate with AI engineers and business stakeholders to design, develop, and deploy LLM-integrated systems.

Projects and Responsibilities:
#1. AI-Powered Knowledge/Data Hub for HR
Objective: Build a centralized HR platform with LLM-driven semantic search (using Llama 4/DeepSeek) for policies, FAQs, and employee data.
Key Tasks:
· Design a vector database (e.g., MongoDB, ArgoDB) for LLM-powered retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
· Fine-tune Llama 4/DeepSeek models for HR-specific Q&A and document summarization.
· Develop a secure web interface (eg. React + FastAPI) with role-based access control.

#2. Financial Report Consolidation with LLM Automation
Objective: Automate extraction and standardization of financial data (PDFs, Excel) using LLM agents (DeepSeek/Llama 4).
Key Tasks:
· Build pipelines to parse tables/text from diverse formats using LLM-based tools (e.g., unstructured.io, Llama Index).
· Train custom models for entity recognition (e.g., invoice numbers, dates) with Llama 4.
· Create a unified dashboard (Plotly, Streamlit) for financial analytics.

Minimum Technical Requirements
Core Skills:
Python/JavaScript, REST APIs, and SQL/NoSQL databases.
Experience with LLM frameworks (LangChain, Llama Index) and model fine-tuning.
Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS/Azure) and containerization (Docker).
Preferred:
Hands-on experience with Llama 4, DeepSeek, or similar open-source LLMs.
Knowledge of vector databases, RAG, or LLM agent design.
Understanding of data privacy (GDPR, SOC 2).
What We Offer
· Dedicated mentorship from AI engineers working with Llama/DeepSeek.
· Access to GPU clusters for model training and deployment.
· Opportunity to publish blog posts/case studies on your project.

Requirements
- Currently at least in Year 2 of studies in a local University in Singapore.
- Mandatory (Credit Bearing) or Non-Credit Bearing Internships.
- Keen on taking on internship for a minimum of 4 months (16 weeks).
skills: Troubleshooting, Dashboard, Pipelines, REST, Application Development, Scripting, Electrical, Administration, Access Control, SQL, Python, Containerization, Java, Databases, FastAPI, Software Development, C++, Technical Support

Job Description:
Nanyang Technological University, Singapore
https://www.ntu.edu.sg/index

A research-intensive public university, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) has 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities, Arts, & Social Sciences, and Graduate colleges. It also has a medical school, the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, established jointly with Imperial College London.

NTU is also home to world-class autonomous institutes – the National Institute of Education, S Rajaratnam School of International Studies, Earth Observatory of Singapore, and Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering – and various leading research centres such as the Nanyang Environment & Water Research Institute (NEWRI) and Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N).
Ranked amongst the world’s top universities by QS, NTU has also been named the world’s top young university for the past seven years. The University’s main campus is frequently listed among the Top 15 most beautiful university campuses in the world and has 57 Green Mark-certified (equivalent to LEED-certified) buildings, of which 95% are certified Green Mark Platinum. Apart from its main campus, NTU also has a campus in Novena, Singapore’s healthcare district.

Under the NTU Smart Campus vision, the University harnesses the power of digital technology and tech-enabled solutions to support better learning and living experiences, the discovery of new knowledge, and the sustainability of resources.

The successful candidate will help foster relationships with local and global Chinese medicine communities, healthcare professionals, and relevant organizations. The successful candidate is expected to participate in public lectures, workshops, and events to promote understanding and awareness of TCM principles and practices.

SBS and CoS seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit on the basis of merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status and family responsibilities, or disability.

Company: AL AMAAN TRADING SG PTE. LTD.
Job Description:
We are looking for an efficient and committed Manager to join our office. As a Manager, you will be managing the day to day activities. You will be responsible for arranging meetings for the staff, provide them necessary official documents and keep a record of the appointments within the office. You will be also arranging monthly repairs and renovations.
You will also be organizing office operations and procedures. You should be highly dedicated to work and have exceptional organizational skills. If you are ready to take up Manager duties and responsibilities, then apply right away. We will love to meet you.
Responsibilities of a Manager
• Work with Human Resources staff to recruit, interview, select, hire, and employ an appropriate number of employees.
• Provide oversight and direction to the employees in the operating unit in accordance with the organization's policies and procedures.
• Health and safety of the workforce.
• Carrying out and attaining the mission and the goals of the business unit managed.
• Development of a superior workforce.
• Development of the department.
• Development of an employee-oriented company culture that emphasizes quality, continuous improvement, key employee retention and development, and high performance.
• Personal ongoing self-development and personal growth as an employee.
• Coach, mentor, and develop staff, including overseeing new employee on boarding and providing career development planning and opportunities.
• Empower employees to take responsibility for their jobs and goals. Delegate responsibility and expect accountability and regular feedback.
• Consciously create a workplace culture that is consistent with the overall organization's and that emphasizes the identified mission, vision, guiding principles, and values of the organization.
• Lead employees using a performance management and development process that provides an overall context and framework to encourage employee contribution and includes goal setting, feedback, and performance development planning.
• Lead employees to meet the organization's expectations for productivity, quality, continuous improvement, and goal accomplishment.
• Provide effective performance feedback through employee recognition, rewards, and disciplinary action, with the assistance of Human Resources, when necessary.
• Maintain employee work schedules including assignments, job rotation, training, vacations, and paid time off, telecommuting, cover for absenteeism, and overtime scheduling.

Job Description:
Nanyang Technological University (NTU) has developed a world-leading position in the fundamental research on quantum and topological photonics, advanced photonic materials and metamaterials. Further development of silicon photonics and advanced Integrated Photonics at NTU is to enable the translation of the results of fundamental studies into technologies on integrated photonics platforms, positioning NTU and Singapore at the forefront of the photonics industry of the future. In this connection, the School of EEE is looking for a visiting professor who has successfully adapted himself/herself to work in the fields of silicon photonics and integrated photonics. The ideal candidate should have strong research experience, achievements and contributions in integrated photonic. He/she is expected to develop strategies for NTU’s Si and Integrated photonic research and contribute to NTU’s effort in bidding for new mega research programs in photonics.

Responsibilities
Work with NTU’s research team to develop strategies for NTU’s integrated photonic research, especially in silicon photonics.
Assist NTU in recruiting promising young scientists and engineers in the area of Si photonic as faculty and research staff.
Enhance NTU’s international visibility and impact by helping to promote NTU, as well as helping the School of EEE establish potentially rewarding international exchanges and contacts that would lead to fruitful international research collaborations and funding.
Contribute to EEE postgraduate programme to train postgraduates in Si photonics which are highly demanded by the industry in recent years.
Requirements
Candidates with the following qualifications are encouraged to apply:
Full professor in silicon photonics and integrated photonic from a reputable university.
At least 10 years of work experience in electrical and electronic engineering, especially in integrated photonics.
More than 5 years of managerial and/or leadership experience in university research.
Good command of verbal and written English.
Application
Applicants are invited to submit their applications (cover letter, CV, research and teaching statements, and citation report, where applicable) through the NTU career portal.
Review of applications and shortlisting decision will be on an on-going basis until the position is filled. Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
skills: Mentoring, University Teaching, Leadership, Silicon Photonics, Written English, Electrical, Electrical Engineering, Silicon, Electronics, Research

Job Description:
The Nanyang Business School, Nanyang Technological University invites applications for faculty positions in Finance at the Assistant/Associate/Full Professor levels

About NTU:
A research-intensive public university, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore (NTU Singapore) has 33,000 undergraduate and postgraduate students in the Engineering, Business, Science, Humanities, Arts, Social Sciences, and Graduate colleges. It also has a medical school, the Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine, established jointly with Imperial College London.

NTU is home to world-class autonomous institutes – the National Institute of Education, S Rajaratnam School of International Studies, Earth Observatory of Singapore, and Singapore Centre for Environmental Life Sciences Engineering – and various leading research centres such as the Nanyang Environment & Water Research Institute (NEWRI) and Energy Research Institute @NTU (ERI@N).

Ranked No. 19 in the most recent QS World University Rankings, NTU has also been named the world’s top young university for the past eight years. The University’s main campus is frequently listed among the Top 15 most beautiful university campuses in the world and has 57 Green Mark-certified (equivalent to LEED-certified) buildings, of which 95% are certified Green Mark Platinum. Apart from its main campus, NTU also has a campus in Novena, Singapore’s healthcare district.

Under the NTU Smart Campus vision, the University aims to harness the power of digital technology and tech-enabled solutions to support better learning and living experiences, the discovery of new knowledge, and the sustainability of resources.

For more information, visit www.ntu.edu.sg.

About the school:
As a premier business school in a leading technological university, Nanyang Business School (NBS) is the nexus of world-class innovation, research, and business education. Situated within NTU’s technology and innovation ecosystem, we are uniquely positioned to create and share knowledge on how cutting-edge technologies can be leveraged for enterprise transformation and a smart economy. Located in Singapore, the heart of East-West culture and business, NBS aims to develop leaders who are able to innovate for the future, lead and transform organizations with cultural dexterity, and build sustainable enterprises in Asia and globally.

NBS is AACSB- and EQUIS-accredited and is currently ranked No. 38 in the 2023 Financial Times (FT) Global MBA ranking and No. 4 in Asia. We have been consistently ranked among the top 40 business schools in the FT Global rankings.

About the Division:
The Division of Banking and Finance (B&F) is committed to research excellence. Based on publications in the top three finance journals over the past five years, B&F ranks No. 47 worldwide and No. 3 in Asia (UTD Top 100 Business School Research Rankings). Please visit our website at
https://www.ntu.edu.sg/business/academic-divisions/banking-finance for more information.

Key Benefits:
NBS offers an internationally competitive salary commensurate with the candidate’s qualifications and experience, as well as funding support for research and conference travels. The University will also provide subsidized on-campus housing for foreign faculty, whereby residents have easy access to modern sports, F&B, childcare, and healthcare facilities in a campus frequently listed among the Top 15 Most Beautiful in the World. Faculty members also enjoy a low-tax environment (tax rate for the first US$100,000 income averages about 10%).

Requirements:
Candidates for the Assistant Professor position should demonstrate great research potential.
Candidates for the Associate Professor position should have an established record of excellence in research and teaching.
Full Professor candidates must demonstrate significant achievements and leadership roles in research and teaching. Candidates must also have made significant contributions to the profession and have great international visibility.
We have multiple positions available, with at least one being reserved for candidates conducting research in “sustainable finance.”
Application:
We encourage seasoned candidates to apply as early as possible. We will contact short-listed candidates only.
To be considered for the position, applicants are invited to formally submit their application materials through the NTU career portal via https://ntu.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/Careers/job/Gaia/Open-Rank-Faculty-Position_R00018348

In addition, we ask rookie applicants to have at least three referees email their reference letters to H-DBF@ntu.edu.sg.
skills: Sustainability, Leadership, Refinance, Teaching, Equity Research, Debt Finance, Research tools, Asset Finance, Trade Finance, Project Finance, Banking, Finance, Islamic Finance, Publications, Group Finance

Storekeeper Duties & Responsibilities:
• Receive and forward all types of goods and deliveries in and out of the hotel to the correct point of storage area.
• Follow all standards for issuing and receiving stock within the store's area of operation.
• Monitor and take inventory on regular basis to compile orders based on par levels or needs.
• Maintain clear and organized records to ensure all reports and invoices are filed and stored properly.
• Monitor Periodic Automatic Replacement levels for all food items to ensure proper levels.
• Responsible to verify all goods arrived as per the agreed purchase, delivery note and agreed quantity has been received.
• Responsible for storage of both food & beverage and operational stock.
• Responsible for the day-to-day check on the storage facilities of upkeep and hygiene.
• Refuse acceptance of damaged, unacceptable, or incorrect items.
• Ensure cleanliness of all areas, keeping storage areas clean & tidy and in strict compliance with hygiene regulations.
• Ensure all store requisitions are signed by concerned Department Heads (HOD’s).
• Ensure the quantity requested and the quantity issued always matches.
• Ensure store requisition form is signed by the person collecting the goods and enter into the Inventory/Materials Management System.
• Post all invoices using the MMS - Material Management System.
• Speak with others using clear and professional language.
• Follow up on documentation of after-hours issues ensuring it is in accordance with established internal controls and procedures.
• Adhere to all Health and Safety procedures particularly relating to food and beverage items.
• Conduct inventory audits to determine inventory levels and needs.
• Assist the Accounts Payable Clerk / payable assistant in finding out any cost discrepancies.
• Complete requisition forms for inventory and supplies.
• Extend all requisitions on a daily basis and update the inventory management software/system.
• Work closely with Purchasing to order and receive items and equipment.
• Troubleshoot any vendor delivery issues and oversee/follow up on the return process.
• Verify and track received inventory and complete inventory reports and logs.
• Perform any other duties as assigned by the management or supervisors.

Storekeeper Requirements and Skills:
• Bachelor’s degree in retail management, marketing, or related fields
• Well experience in retail management or sales
• Knowledge of retail operations, merchandising, and budgeting processes
• Proven ability to develop and implement retail promotion strategies
• Positive, friendly, and organized
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
• Proficient in Microsoft Office.
• The ability to work under pressure.
• Availability to work within opening hours (e.g. evenings, holidays, weekends).
skills: Accounts Payable, Microsoft Office, Housekeeping, Inventory, Purchasing, Unloading, Merchandising, Data Entry, Internal Controls, Inventory Management, Audits, Budgeting, Staff Management, Requisition

Job Description:
The National Institute of Education invites suitable applications for the position of Research Associate on a 12-month contract at the Psychology and Child & Human Development Department.

Project Title: Enhancing Literacy Outcomes for Students with Autism: A Pilot Study of the READ-ASD Program in Singapore

Project Introduction: This project is a research initiative focused on improving reading comprehension outcomes for students with ASD in Singapore through a structured, evidence-based Tier 3 intervention. The project aims to evaluate the effectiveness and feasibility of the READ-ASD intervention in Singapore. This study contributes to culturally responsive literacy practices for children with ASD and supports equitable access to high-quality instruction.

Requirements:
A Master’s degree in Psychology, Education, Special Education, Applied Behavior Analysis, Speech and Language Therapy, or a related field
Experience working with children with autism spectrum disorder (ASD), preferably in educational or intervention settings
Strong organizational and communication skills
Ability to work independently and as part of a research team
Familiarity with basic qualitative and single-case design research methods
Prior research experience and knowledge of reading interventions or applied behavioral analytic approaches is an advantage
Responsibilities:
Support the implementation of the READ-ASD intervention in the participating school
Conduct data collection, including direct observations, interviews, and assessments
Assist in the recruitment and coordination of participants (students, educators, caregivers)
Support transcription, coding, and preliminary analysis of data
Maintain accurate documentation and manage project records in accordance with research ethics requirements
Contribute to literature reviews, preparation of reports, and dissemination activities (e.g., presentations, manuscripts)
Other research tasks assigned by the PI
Application
Applicants (external and internal) will apply via Workday. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Closing Date
Closing date for advertisements will be set to 14 calendar days from date of posting.
skills: Literature Reviews, Psychology, Ability To Work Independently, Literacy, Special Education, Applied Behavior Analysis, Interventions, Communication Skills, Design Research, Transcription

Job Description:
“The Future of Finance, Powered by AI” We're embarking on an exciting journey at Trustplus AI Pte. Ltd. (“TrustPlus AI”), a dynamic tech startup poised to revolutionize credit assessment process for financial institutions through AI. We're currently seeking a visionary individual to join our engineering team, playing a pivotal role in shaping our technology landscape and driving the development of our product lines.

About Us:
TrustPlus AI does what human credit committees take weeks to accomplish — delivering results in seconds with even greater accuracy. Founded by Kevin Lee and WayVern Shong, former credit executives at leading global financial institutions, TrustPlus AI started on the belief that modern AI can now outperform traditional human analysis in credit evaluation – and we're proving it. Our agentic platform instantly crunches more data than any human ever could, distilling thousands of data points into a single, precise credit decision – a technical moat that deepens with every credit assessment processing. In multi-trillion-dollar lending and payment markets hungry for innovation, TrustPlus AI is uniquely positioned to redefine how creditors assess risk, backed by a defensible AI advantage and deep domain expertise.

Expansion Plans:
But that's not where our journey ends. We have a bold vision to expand our product offerings beyond credit and risk management. Our roadmap includes venturing into compliance, legal, operations, and other critical areas within the financial ecosystem.

Role Overview:
We are looking for a curious and driven AI Engineer Intern who is passionate about the intersection of AI and FinTech. You’ll support research and development efforts, assist in real-world projects, and learn directly from experienced engineers in a dynamic, startup environment.

Responsibilities:
· Support the development and implementation of NLP and LLM models for our AI-powered financial products.
· Assist in research and prototyping of LLM, RAG, and other AI techniques to enhance our credit risk solutions.
· Collaborate with the engineering team to collect, clean, and preprocess data for machine learning tasks.
· Help evaluate model performance and conduct experiments to improve accuracy, efficiency, and scalability.
· Stay current with the latest research and advancements in AI, ML, and NLP, and share insights with the team.
· Participate in team meetings, code reviews, and discussions to contribute ideas and grow technical expertise.

Qualifications:
· Currently pursuing or recently completed a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related field.
· Strong interest in AI/ML, especially NLP and LLM technologies (e.g., GPT, BERT, RAG, etc.).
· Proficiency in Python and a basic understanding of machine learning libraries such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or Hugging Face.
· Experience with data preprocessing, model training, or related coursework/projects is a plus.
· Self-motivated, eager to learn, and comfortable working in a fast-paced startup environment.
· Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively in a multicultural, remote-first team.

Job Description:
NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory is a collaboration between Nanofilm Technologies International Limited (“Nanofilm”, “NTI”), Nanyang Technological University (“NTU”) and supported by Singapore under RIE2025. The Laboratory’s objective is to propel Innovation and Technologies commercialisation through NTU’s innovation and NTI’s deep technology. NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory aligns with Singapore’s RIE2025 handbook – which emphasises the nation’s commitment to research and innovation, aiming to drive economic growth and address national challenges.

The Nanyang Technological University NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory is seeking to hire a Research Fellow. The selected candidate will innovate hybridized composite materials and thin film coatings.

Key Responsibilities:
Simulate, design, fabricate and characterize plasma process equipment.
Familiar with COMSOL software to simulate the influence of magnetic field on plasma flow.
Conducts various design simulations to refine processes.
Optimize vacuum coating processes, and leading R&D efforts in material science for application development.
Drives the development and implementation of coating techniques.
Oversee the transition from experimental designs to scalable solutions.
Additionally, the role requires preparation and delivery of comprehensive reports to satisfy the stipulations of funding entities and corporate mandates.
Job Requirements:
Ph.D. in a relevant engineering or science discipline is required.
At least 2 years of combined specialized post-doctoral experience in industry or a recognized research institute.
Expertise in plasma physics, plasma source design, plasma control system design and a strong track record of research publications and patent filings are essential.
Applicants must demonstrate effective collaboration skills in multidisciplinary settings.
We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Description:
NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory is a collaboration between Nanofilm Technologies International Limited (“Nanofilm”, “NTI”), Nanyang Technological University (“NTU”) and supported by Singapore under RIE2025. The Laboratory’s objective is to propel Innovation and Technologies commercialisation through NTU’s innovation and NTI’s deep technology. NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory aligns with Singapore’s RIE2025 handbook – which emphasises the nation’s commitment to research and innovation, aiming to drive economic growth and address national challenges.

The Nanyang Technological University NTI-NTU Corporate Laboratory is seeking an accomplished and forward-thinking Senior Research Fellow to play a leading role in the development of PVD/CVD-based multi-functional composite thin film coatings. This is a unique opportunity to join a high-impact research team focused on pioneering next-generation materials with strong industrial relevance across electronics, aerospace, automotive, and energy sectors.

The successful candidate will take technical ownership of thin film deposition, materials characterization, and process innovation, contributing both strategic insight and hands-on leadership to research programs at the forefront of plasma processing and surface engineering.

Key Responsibilities:
Lead the design, optimization, and scale-up of PVD and CVD plasma-based processes, with an emphasis on high-performance composite coatings e.g. oxide and nitride.
Drive the comprehensive characterization of thin films using advanced techniques (e.g., SEM, XRD, Raman, AFM, nanoindentation), enabling deep understanding of structure-property relationships.
Spearhead independent, hypothesis-driven experimental research, and interpret complex datasets to extract actionable insights for process refinement and material enhancement.
Apply advanced data analysis methods, simulation tools, and scientific programming to support process modeling and predictive materials design.
Translate experimental findings into scalable, commercially viable processes, working closely with internal teams and industrial partners to align with product development goals.
Author and present detailed technical reports, white papers, and research documentation to support grant deliverables, IP generation, and strategic decision-making.
Collaborate across disciplines, mentor junior researchers, and contribute to a culture of technical excellence and continuous innovation.
job Requirements:
Ph.D. in Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, Physics, or a closely related field.
At least 5 years of combined specialized post-doctoral experience in industry or a recognized research institute.
Extensive research experience in the characterization of thin film composite coatings, with a strong emphasis on oxide and nitride composite thin film coatings.
Applicants must demonstrate effective collaboration skills in multidisciplinary settings.
We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Description:
We are seeking an experienced Linux Engineer to join our IT Infrastructure team. The ideal candidate will have strong expertise in administering Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) systems in an on-premises environment, with a solid background in systems security, performance tuning, automation, and virtualization. This role demands proactive system monitoring, hands-on experience with clustering, and a strong ability to respond to and manage security incidents effectively.

Install, configure, and maintain RHEL servers, ensuring system security, performance, and high availability.
Monitor and troubleshoot Linux infrastructure across networking, storage, and security components.
Design and implement anti-virus solutions (e.g., SEP, McAfee) for malware protection and system integrity.
Respond to malware incidents, executing containment, eradication, and recovery protocols.
Analyze operational performance and introduce infrastructure enhancements for improved reliability and efficiency.
Develop and maintain Ansible-based configuration management and automation scripts to streamline system administration tasks.
Collaborate with application teams for seamless integration of Linux systems with business-critical services.
Enforce compliance with internal security policies, industry standards, and regulatory frameworks.

Handle service requests, change management, and maintain system uptime standards.
Administer and support VMware environments integrated with Linux infrastructure.
Coordinate with cross-functional teams and external vendors to ensure smooth operations.
Provide 24x7 support, participate in on-call rotations, and contribute to system reliability.
Offer technical guidance to project teams and stakeholders on Linux-based infrastructure solutions.
Maintain up-to-date documentation and configuration records.

8+ years of hands-on experience as a Linux/Unix System
Expert knowledge of RHEL administration, including system performance, patching, and troubleshooting.
Strong experience with clustering technologies (e.g., Pacemaker), VMware, and storage/SAN administration.
In-depth knowledge of anti-virus solutions and their integration with Linux platforms.
Proficiency in automation and configuration management tools, especially Ansible.
Understanding of ITIL or other ITSM frameworks is a plus.

Job Description:
Job Title: UI/UX Design Intern

Location: Singapore

Company Overview:
Adactive Asia is a leading provider of AI-driven wayfinding solutions and dynamic content management systems. We specialize in creating innovative, user-centric applications that integrate cutting-edge technologies for commercial spaces, including shopping malls and public venues. Our mission is to simplify navigation and enhance user experience through intelligent design and intuitive interfaces.
Internship Overview:
We are looking for a creative and detail-oriented UI/UX Design Intern to join our dynamic design team. In this role, you will collaborate closely with our developers and product managers to create seamless, user-friendly interfaces and experiences. This internship offers an exciting opportunity to contribute to the design of intuitive and engaging web applications, while gaining practical experience in a fast-paced, technology-driven environment.
Responsibilities:
Assist in the design and development of user interfaces for web applications and mobile platforms.
Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity mockups for new features and improvements.
Conduct user research and usability testing to gather feedback and improve design decisions.
Work closely with the development team to ensure designs are implemented accurately and efficiently.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand user needs and business goals, translating them into effective design solutions.
Assist in maintaining and updating the design system to ensure consistency across all platforms.
Continuously learn and stay updated with design trends, tools, and best practices.
Job Requirements:
Experience:
Currently pursuing a Diploma or Degree in UI/UX Design, Interaction Design, Graphic Design, or a related field.
Proficiency in design tools such as Adobe XD, Figma, Sketch, or similar software.
Basic understanding of design principles, typography, color theory, and layout.
Familiarity with wireframing and prototyping tools.
Previous experience with web and mobile design (personal projects or coursework) is a plus.
Knowledge of HTML/CSS is beneficial, but not required.
Character and Attitude:
Strong interest in user-centered design and improving user experiences.
A proactive attitude with the ability to learn quickly and take initiative.
Creative and innovative, with the ability to think outside the box while still keeping the user’s needs in mind.
Detail-oriented with excellent problem-solving skills.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to present and explain design concepts effectively.
Collaborative and able to work well in a team setting, as well as independently.
Willingness to receive and act upon feedback to improve design work.
Passionate about keeping up-to-date with the latest design trends and technologies.
What We Offer:
Hands-on experience with real-world design projects that impact users in commercial spaces.
Mentorship from experienced UI/UX designers and cross-functional team members.
Flexible working hours to accommodate your academic schedule.
A dynamic and creative work environment with opportunities to contribute new ideas.
Exposure to industry-standard design tools and technologies.
Duration: 3-6 months (flexible based on academic requirements)
How to Apply:
Interested candidates should submit their resume, portfolio (or design samples), and a brief cover letter outlining why they are interested in the internship to [contact@adactive.asia].

About Us
Found across 75 countries & territories, and with more than 75 years of history, Manpower is the global leader in contingent and permanent staffing, providing organizations of all sizes with a continuum of staffing solutions to enhance business agility and competitiveness.
We are committed to helping people adapt for future jobs, which extends beyond our employees to associates and candidates. Our workforce solutions increasingly position us as a creator of talent at scale where we provide learning, development, and upskilling opportunities through our ManpowerGroup Academies.

The successful incumbent can look forward to being part of the exciting team where we work as an Expert to share with clients the current job trends and best ways to attract candidates while creating a positive hiring experience for all candidates through assessment, data and coaching, so that they can provide candidates personalized guidance to develop for future roles.

Determine potential applicant profile by studying job descriptions and job requirements

Build a wide network with industry professionals through networking
